recommendationsReceived.name: Amanda Weigel
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Director, Medicare Advantage Product Strategy at UnitedHealth Group
recommendationsReceived.context: Amanda was senior to Dayna but didn't manage Dayna directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Dayna is a talented and passionate marketer – while working with her at General Mills she showed great passion for reaching consumers via social media and understood the role each channel played in the larger communication strategy. In addition to the day-to-day management of many social channels for two large cereal brands,  Dayna took the initiative to dig into performance metrics and audience data. She operationalized reporting and began overlaying insights that were reported back to the brand managers. This process allowed her to quickly gain the trust of her team, allowing an easy “yes” to her recommendations. As the role of social media grew in importance and new platforms emerged, Dayna quickly got up-to-speed and evolved our content strategy and distribution plans accordingly. I highly recommend Dayna and hope to work with her again in the future.

recommendations.name: Amanda Lee
recommendations.subtitle: Brand Design Leader | Design Strategist | Design | Brand Design | Brand Building | Design Strategy | Brand Strategy | Package Design | CPG
recommendations.context: Dayna was senior to Amanda but didn't manage Amanda directly
recommendations.description:
I've had the pleasure of working with Amanda for over five years, and I'm consistently impressed by her design strategy, ability to juggle multiple objectives and manage multiple stakeholders (both internal and external partners). She's a true leader when it comes to packaging design, guiding our team to create impactful designs that clearly communicate key information on-pack. For example, we’ve worked across multiple Event packaging projects where she’s navigated feedback across each Operating Unit (Cereal, Snacks & Meals), along with multiple partners (e.g. movie studio, Hershey’s, etc.). 
Another example is when Amanda was instrumental in the successful launch of new Lucky Charms chapters, like the Dragon and Magic Gem editions. She expertly balanced the need to maintain brand recognition with the excitement of new product news, creating packaging that truly drew consumers to the shelf. Her deep understanding of design principles and the critical "first moment of truth" for CPG brands directly contributed to strong business results. Amanda is a valuable asset to any team!

recommendations.name: Rudy Arnold
recommendations.subtitle: Creative / Co-Founder @ Mulligan | Bachelor of Fine Arts
recommendations.context: Dayna managed Rudy directly
recommendations.description: Rudy is a top tier designer and photographer. I had the pleasure of working with him during his time contracting for General Mills on the brand Cinnamon Toast Crunch. Rudy juggled many social media projects all while remaining extremely efficient and always delivering the creative on time. I was also very impressed by his brainstorming ability and coming up with creative solutions to any idea. It's such a fun brand and Rudy really brought the fun/craziness to life. I'd highly recommend Rudy and think he'd be an asset to any team.

This leads to a brilliant, on-brief creative.

recommendations.name: Amanda Weigel
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Director, Medicare Advantage Product Strategy at UnitedHealth Group
recommendations.context: Amanda was senior to Dayna but didn't manage Dayna directly
recommendations.description: It has truly been one of the most rewarding experiences working with Amanda. When our team was in disarray due to staffing changes, she proactively transitioned herself to take on managerial duties. Amanda’s ability to provide structure, purpose and encouragement brought out my best work. Her thoughtfulness and foresight guided not only me, but also our entire team. https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAK5AJgB.headline: Marketer |  Storyteller |  Facilitator | Culture Creator | Data Strategist
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAK5AJgB.about:
“I get to choose the toy that goes in the cereal box!!!”  That’s how Tiffany Carver described her role to friends and family when she first started her career with General Mills in 1999.  Upon graduating from McMaster University, Tiffany knew that her future lay in the new and different, and that’s exactly what her first role at the cereal giant had to offer.  Today, Tiffany leads General Mills’ Shopper Solutions team, pioneering the idea of ‘partnership for change’ and successfully leading a talented team devoted to the integrated marketing of General Mills' leading brands to today's shoppers. 

Tiffany always finds herself at the epicenter of change. Whether inside the walls of General Mills or as a trusted advisor at the customer’s table, she’s always at the top of the batting order for anyone facing a new challenge.  Known for her passion and candour, Tiffany has pushed the envelope on more than one occasion to deliver some of the best-loved promotions and integrated campaigns in the industry.  In addition to being a go-to facilitator for innovative thinking, Tiffany is a strong supporter of women in grocery and serves on the women's leadership network for General Mills.  Tiffany’s commitment to creativity, leadership, and results has been recognized by the Canadian grocery industry with a Star Women in Grocery Award. 

A lot has changed in her 20+ years with General Mills, including the toys inside the cereal box.  What has never wavered is Tiffany’s out of the box thinking that will continue to shake the industry.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAADLHutkB.headline: Brand Experience Manager, Old El Paso 🌮
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAADLHutkB.about:
I’m an ambitious brand builder with over two years’ experience driving results for global FMCG brands; Dove, Lifebuoy, Häagen-Dazs, and Old El Paso. My background spans both local (UK) and global roles. Globally, I’ve played a key role in developing 360° campaigns, building brand worlds, executing full-portfolio multi-market packaging redesigns and rollout plans, and creating TTL content for diverse markets. Locally, I’ve led limited edition projects from pack design to sell-ins and comms support, managed budgets and performance analysis, and handled retailer sell-ins, PR, and major partnership initiatives. Colleagues recognise me for bringing clarity, energy, and results to every project and team.

My marketing journey began with a year-long placement at Unilever, working on personal care brands in the UK and Ireland. This experience brought my academic interests to life and deepened my commitment to a marketing career. Since then, I have held two international roles—first as Assistant Brand Manager for Häagen-Dazs, before being promoted to Brand Experience Manager for Old El Paso, where I work in a lean team and have the opportunity to lead and support on big bet projects. I’m a keen team player, committed to building positive team culture, whether planning team days or leading as a pillar within the General Mills Women’s Leadership Network in UKI.

I graduated from Durham University with First-Class Honours in Business & Management, achieving 82% for my dissertation, “Behind the Refill Movement”, which explored how FMCG professionals market refill products in the UK. Alongside my studies, I played an active role in student life, honing my communication, leadership, and relationship-building skills through society leadership. I remain engaged with the Durham community as a mentor to two students via the DU Women in Business Society.

Passionate about consumer-centric marketing, I’m always seeking to understand consumer behaviour and find new ways to drive growth. I’m energised by creativity, strategic thinking, and collaborating with curious, driven people—these are the environments where I believe the most can be learned.

updates.postText:
I am proud to have graduated from Durham University with a First Class Honours degree in Business & Management with a Placement Year. Thank you to my family, friends and academic staff at Durham University Business School who supported me throughout. 

My dissertation, 'Behind the Refill Movement: A Phenomenological Exploration into FMCG Professionals’ Experience and Perceptions of Marketing Practices for Refills in the UK’ was inspired by the work I did during my placement year at Unilever. I am deeply grateful to the FMCG professionals who took the time to support me in this endeavour. I thoroughly enjoyed interviewing each of you, your contributions were truly insightful. I was delighted to be awarded 82% on this piece of work.

Although I am sad to conclude my time at John Snow College  Durham University, I am excited to begin my graduate career as an Associate Marketing Manager at General Mills, where I will be working on the Häagen-Dazs brand.

And just like that, it’s over! 

Yesterday marked the end of my unforgettable placement year at Unilever  and I couldn't have asked for a better experience! Working on both the Lifebuoy and Dove Skin Cleansing (UK) teams, here are some of my highlights: 

💭 Being a brand lead on a value channel exclusive, limited edition range for Dove skin cleansing & deodorants, collaborating cross-functionally to bring it to life.

🛁 Planning internal PR for the Dove Shower re-launch, raising awareness and introducing colleagues to the derma hypoallergenic variant.

🛒 Locally optimising visuals for Dove shower POSM and supporting the shopper plans for three key retailers.

📊 Analysing brand performance, developing strong business cases, and presenting to key stakeholders.

⚽️ 🏏 🎶 Managing Lifebuoy’s partnerships with the EFL, ECB, The Hundred, and The O2 Arena!

📱 Managing social media for Lifebuoy UKI, leading engaging competitions, and sharing co-created content across platforms.

🎤 Presenting 2023 Unilever liquid hand wash plans to two retailers.

🧼 Working on brand activations like the Goodwood Festival and Global Handwashing Day 2022.

The Unilever culture has been incredible, filled with joy and purpose. I was voted Q1 Personal Care ‘All Star Player of the Season’ 🏆, took part in charity walks, volunteered at a charity football match 🥅, contributed to the Outreach Team, and helped plan team away days ⛳️.

A big thank you to everyone who supported my  development throughout this year. Special shout-outs to Emily Galazka, Emily Godding, Morgan McAuley, Charlotte Alexander, Gracie Mallett and Team Skin Cleanse for their invaluable guidance. I'll miss you all!

Although I am sad to be leaving, I look forward to applying the experiences in my final year at Durham University.

Great to be back at Durham University  today for the Business School Placement Showcase. I loved chatting to all of the students interested in the future careers opportunities at Unilever!  

Thanks Alexandra McNinch for organising and inviting us up for this event! 

 Mimi Milligan-Manby James Hamilton-Jones Abigail Bennett 🤍

https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAACFF6AB.about:
I’m a proud Chinese, Brazilian, Minnesotan. I am passionate about educating, inspiring, and creating more moments for real connection among all humans – no matter who you are or where you come from. My desire is to leave this planet in a better place than I found it.

I am most proud about the coaching, development, and relationships I have invested in and built throughout my career. With fantastic teams, I have created and scaled new brands, turned-around struggling brands, and grown large scale multi-million-dollar brands. I have led many cross-functional teams to achieve things greater than we thought possible, and I’ve also been a change agent and trusted source of support throughout challenging times. I have experience in identifying, growing, and retaining top talent and building strong community and culture. I am energized by creating winning teams that can quickly connect dots to generate growth opportunities & strategies. 

I want to make an impact, together, as a community and team.

experiences.title: Senior Marketing Manager
experiences.jobDescription:
KID CEREALS: Accountable for Strategy, Growth, & Profit, $500M in Sales. Led direct team of 5 & cross-functional team of 20.
•	Over-delivered growth on Lucky Charms and grew Net Sales by +$35M, leading Brand Marketing & Marketing Communications teams to create & execute a Marshmallows Only on-pack & in-store promotional giveaway and the inaugural Magical Possibilities kid & adult co-viewing campaign. 
•	Led Cross-functional team to identify new growth consumer targets for Reese's Puffs, Cocoa Puffs, Trix, and Kix. Grew Reese's Puffs +$20M in Net Sales by developing a partnership with the NBA and new in-store seasonal strategy. Doubled Trix brand by $70M in Net Sales by returning the product back to its Classic recipe.

YOPLAIT YOGURT: Accountable for Strategy, Growth, & Profit, $100M in Sales. Led direct team of 6 & cross-functional team of 15.
•	Developed strategic brand re-positioning of the Yoplait Greek brand to stabilize & reverse significant declines. Piloted and built new intensive, speed-to-market agile process in partnership with Innovation Center of Excellence to transform the product and packaging within 7 months. 
•	Received 2016 President’s Award for Collaboration & Impact. 

DISRUPTIVE CEREAL INNOVATION: Accountable for Creating New Category Expansion. Led direct team of 5 & cross-functional team of 15. 
•	Successfully built $100M in Net Sales of new granola business by creating Autumn’s Gold Grain Free Granola, the first, new sustainable Cereal and Bars brand for General Mills in 20 years. 
•	Created a winning, entrepreneurial culture within General Mills by crafting a clear vision & process for developing innovation in the fast-paced Natural/Organic food segment.
•	Awarded 2015 Cereal Innovation of the Year.

updates.postText: So proud of the excellent work that our teams around the world & BBH London have done to showcase that Hӓagen-Dazs continues to raise the bar to provide the very best taste & quality. Congrats and many thanks to Bronwyn C. Kaylee Pohlmeyer Jade Evans Maria Huete Françoise BECHU Love seeing this come to life in markets across Europe, Asia, Latin America, the Middle East, and in Häagen-Dazs shops. Excited for this and more to come! #iworkforgeneralmills

1 year ago, I had a big personal life change, moved across the globe to the UK, and took on the role of Global Head of the Häagen-Dazs brand. Thanks to many who have believed in me at General Mills, I have the great opportunity to lead this brand globally. I feel fortunate to work alongside talented teams around the world, and I have learned so much in the last year working across markets and cultures. One of my biggest learnings has been that there is more that unites us than divides us, especially when it comes to food.

Häagen-Dazs is a brand that was founded to become the world's best ice cream, with a relentless commitment to the highest quality and craft standards. Proud to work on a brand that has been so consistent over time and around the world. Looking forward to continuing this great legacy. #iwork4generalmills

recommendationsReceived.name: Alexandra Kokot
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Assistant Vice President @ NYCEDC | Driving innovation in NYC's Green Economy & revitalizing real estate assets through adaptive reuse
recommendationsReceived.context: Alexandra reported directly to Priscilla
recommendationsReceived.description: Being on Priscilla's team at Annie's was transformative. Priscilla's energy is electric - she inspires passion, drive, and the will to win within her team. Priscilla entrusts her team with significant responsibility while providing the expert guidance that allowed us to bring challenging launches and high profile initiatives across the finish line. She is a seasoned strategist who encouraged us to develop creative, disruptive plans and advanced them with vigor. Priscilla led our team with clear strategic intent, always provided transparency and cultivated strong relationships so that her team could successfully navigate the organization. She went above and beyond in fostering my professional and personal development, prioritizing dedicated 1:1 coaching sessions and was the #1 champion of community initiatives I led at Annie's. Priscilla is an incredible advocate who regularly puts her team in front of leadership and ensures that they see and recognize her team members' contributions.  From team lunch outside to group cooking class, Priscilla made bonding time a priority - there were a lot of laughs and a lot to celebrate because we were such an effective group. I would absolutely love to work for Priscilla again and know that I can count on her wisdom as I grow my career!

recommendationsReceived.name: Arne Panzer
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Manager, Customer Strategy
recommendationsReceived.context: Arne reported directly to Priscilla
recommendationsReceived.description:
I can say that Priscilla has, without a doubt, been the most impactful manager and leader I have worked with. She has contributed more to my personal and professional growth than anyone I’ve ever worked for, investing in my development as a top priority. 
Her vision and clarity to transform a business and her drive to push this vision through the organization are second to none. She does so by being deeply connected to her cross-functional peer group as well as her key stakeholders. She is a tireless champion for her team, enabling growth opportunities through stretch assignments, providing thoughtful and actionable feedback, and advocating for her teams’ success. 
All the while, she prioritizes the team dynamic, fostering a culture of community and friendship among all members. I have had the opportunity to work on Priscilla’s team on 2 separate occasions and hope to one day have the opportunity to work for her again.

recommendationsReceived.name: Devin Kunysz
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: GTM Leader For Consumer Goods & Retail at Salesforce. Deep consumer goods and retail expertise. Delivering the future of AI & CRM. I can tell you where the puck is headed on AI Agents for Consumer & Retail
recommendationsReceived.context: Devin reported directly to Priscilla
recommendationsReceived.description:
Priscilla led one of the highest performing teams I have had the pleasure of working on. She was an outstanding leader who empowered each of us to think differently, challenged our expectations and drove smart priorities.

I reported directly to her for more than a year while we overhauled General Mills sweet cereal portfolio for growth. She was an outstanding leader and made coming to work a true joy. 

Our team not only turned the business from a multi year decline to double digit growth, but we also all ate lunch together every day. She built a winning culture and a team that still stays in touch. I have used what she taught me about brand management almost every day and was the first person i reached out to for coaching as i took on a team of my own.

experiences.title: Manager, Marketing Activation & Operations
experiences.jobDescription:
I lead our Marketing Experiences / Brand Experience Operations team, consisting of Marketing Governance, Kitchens & Photography and Digital Asset Management. Our group:

-Leads central policy and technology Operations & Governance for the Brand Experience function by facilitating streamlined processes that can be replicated throughout the organization.

-Centrally manages digital assets across the organization, providing user support, training and ensuring asset lifecycle maintenance to drive consistent DAM use. 

-Provides food expertise and partnership across internally & externally created recipes, photo and other food-related content and serve as the backbone of recipe management via RMS.

experiences.title: Marketing Experience Strategy - Risk Management & Governance
experiences.jobDescription:
- Protect the integrity and safety of our consumers and brands across a wide range of promotional activations, particularly higher-risk programs involving sweepstakes, contests, premiums and product sampling

- Serve as subject matter expert to support Brand Experience teams across as they concept, develop and execute brand-building promotional programs, including training and education as part of the employee onboarding and rotation process; track and monitor promotional activations to ensure compliance with our marketing policies, applicable laws and company- and industry-wide best practices, including serving on Marketing Technology Approval team

- Interface with cross-functional business partners - Law, Sourcing, R&D, Global Business Solutions - to provide appropriate controls and guardrails across our promotional plans and executions

- Qualify, manage and oversee Brand Experience external agency partners to ensure they meet or exceed our risk management requirements and expectations

- Provide central oversight and governance across General Mills U.S. social media footprint and maintain a list of social media accounts/presences across all U.S. brands and monitor user access across these accounts and the third-party tools used in social media execution

- Serve as the Brand Experience lead for Holistic Margin Management and provide regular updates to the leadership team on progress against annual targets

- Lead a cross-functional coalition including Sourcing, Finance and Global Business Solutions, to build, track and manage a robust pipeline of Holistic Margin Management projects related to Marketing Communications capabilities and investment

experiences.title: Program Manager, Creative Project Management
experiences.jobDescription:
- Provide structure and project management across large retainer clients and custom projects with global scope, high risk and/or complexity. Extensive experience with email, print, digital, strategy, social and brand development. Participate in cross-organization initiatives and RFPs.

- Identify creative, client service, strategy, analytics, development and project management staffing needs for collection of projects and work with resource management as needed to secure additional resources. Manage dozens of projects on various timelines from scoping through creative development and final delivery.

- Interface with all internal teams and executive management to assure objectives are understood and agency's work addresses the business requirements. Ensure SOWs for work within the program are consistent, comprehensive, accurate and protect the interests of the agency. Deliver financial management of the accounts and identify opportunities and drive efficiencies leading to improved account margins.  

- Retain a solid understanding of the client’s business, expectations and requirements. Collaborate with the client service team in determining the approach and deliverables. volunteerAndAwards.description:
The Bean Path essentially provides technical advice and guidance to individuals and small businesses in the community. Our initiatives include tech hours at the local libraries, engineering and coding programs for youth, and scholarships/grants for students and community organizations.

Our mission is to sow technical expertise in order to grow networks and fertilize communities.

www.beanpath.org

The New York Urban League Young Professionals (NYULYP) is a unique entity of the New York Urban League designed to serve as an empowerment forum for individuals ages twenty-one to forty that live and work throughout New York City.

This premier civil rights auxiliary group works to promote and support the mission of the New York Urban League by training the next generation of leaders through volunteer opportunities, personal and professional development and fund development.

Founded April 1, 2003, the NYULYP is one of 62 chapters of the National Urban League Young Professionals. This deeply rooted movement serves as a network of young professionals across the country that works to eradicate social and economic inequalities through the local implementation of the National Urban League’s empowerment agenda.

Black Girls CODE is devoted to showing the world that black girls can code, and do so much more. By reaching out to the community through workshops and after school programs, Black Girls CODE introduces computer coding lessons to young girls from underrepresented communities in programming languages such as Scratch or Ruby on Rails. Black Girls CODE has set out to prove to the world that girls of every color have the skills to become the programmers of tomorrow. By promoting classes and programs we hope to grow the number of women of color working in technology and give underprivileged girls a chance to become the masters of their technological worlds. Black Girls CODE's ultimate goal is to provide African-American youth with the skills to occupy some of the 1.4 million computing job openings expected to be available in the U.S. by 2020, and to train 1 million girls by 2040.

CIN inspires, connects and strengthens African Americans and communities of color to leverage their collective resources and create the change THEY wish to see.
CIN is a national network of giving circles. A giving circle is a group who pool their personal time, talents, and treasure to address social issues in their communities using thoughtful giving strategies. The activities of a giving circle include grant making, advocacy and public education as complementary ways to demystify philanthropy by making it accessible and more democratic to everyday people. CIN understands the importance of guiding African Americans and people of color to the supply side of philanthropy and to be the first investors in their communities.

recommendationsReceived.name: Nu Martin
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Commercial Associate at General Mills
recommendationsReceived.context: Nu worked with Amanda on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Amanda has been such a wonderful person to have as a teammate. She is welcoming, inclusive, humorous and has an exceptionally creative vision for design. Collaborating with Amanda is always a pleasure as she consistently challenges me to expand a my creative lens through her expertise in Brand Design. She approaches each project with thoughtfulness and creativity, bringing both strategic insight and clear direction. Not only does Amanda bring heart and dedication to everything she does, but also a strong wealth of knowledge that elevates the work.

recommendationsReceived.description: I had the chance to work with Amanda in a cross-functional role between Blue Buffalo and General Mills, and she was truly a standout partner. She has a sharp eye for detail and a strong design instinct that consistently raised the bar on everything we worked on, from initial briefs to final execution. Amanda brings a great balance of knowledge, planning, creative vision and hands-on dedication, and she’s the kind of person who makes collaboration easy. Reliable, thoughtful, and a genuine team player who brings out the best in those around her.

recommendationsReceived.name: Dayna Needham
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Portfolio Events Brand Experience Manager at General Mills
recommendationsReceived.context: Dayna was senior to Amanda but didn't manage Amanda directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
I've had the pleasure of working with Amanda for over five years, and I'm consistently impressed by her design strategy, ability to juggle multiple objectives and manage multiple stakeholders (both internal and external partners). She's a true leader when it comes to packaging design, guiding our team to create impactful designs that clearly communicate key information on-pack. For example, we’ve worked across multiple Event packaging projects where she’s navigated feedback across each Operating Unit (Cereal, Snacks & Meals), along with multiple partners (e.g. movie studio, Hershey’s, etc.). 
Another example is when Amanda was instrumental in the successful launch of new Lucky Charms chapters, like the Dragon and Magic Gem editions. She expertly balanced the need to maintain brand recognition with the excitement of new product news, creating packaging that truly drew consumers to the shelf. Her deep understanding of design principles and the critical "first moment of truth" for CPG brands directly contributed to strong business results. Amanda is a valuable asset to any team!

She is clear on her vision, and knows exactly how to push for strong ideas in a way that keeps everyone feeling energized. You always know where things stand with Amanda, and her positivity, focus and quick problem solving keeps the momentum going!

Amanda is incredibly passionate and fully invested in doing great work, all while keeping the process fun (which is no small feat). Working with Amanda has been an absolute pleasure, and any team would be lucky to have her!

She is a team player. As cliche as that statement may be, Kim oversaw EVERY General Mills project that ran through our company in some fashion, so being able to manage that amount of work on top of several different personalities who all believe their project is the hottest - she takes the gold for that event.

She has a great offensive game. Rather than coming to our team with issues, Kim anticipated them before they could arise and suggested ways around them or ways to combat them. Our team greatly appreciated this provocativeness as it saved us from unnecessary churn and improved our bottom line.

All said and done, Kim is an all-star. She can handle any task that thrown her way, and will beat your expectations with ease.

updates.postText:
Did we just land on the same list as the Backstreet Boys?!🎤🔥 Our Oatmeal Crisp Protein sampling event made BizBash’s ‘Cool Event Ideas’ for July. Looks like breakfast is back, alright! 😏

Jenny Chiasson Samantha Martin Safiya Abbadi Derek DeNure Anushka Gilani Lisa Nanakdewa

Introducing our Oatmeal Crisp “Protein Power Team!”🥣💪

We wanted to launch OC Protein in a big way so alongside our media campaign, we hit the streets of Toronto last week for a high-energy sampling stunt.

Free samples, photo ops with the OC Protein Power Team, and a little bit of organized chaos (thanks to our influencer friends and Narcity) made this launch one to remember.

And we’re not done yet! Montreal, we’re coming for you later this week!

Huge thanks to House of XM for bringing the sampling stunt to life, and to Craft Public Relations for the buzz-worthy PR!

#OCProtein #iworkforgenmills 

Jenny Chiasson Anushka Gilani Lisa Nanakdewa Safiya Abbadi Derek DeNure Samantha Martin

Feed your Creativity with Reese’s Puffs cereal! We wanted to connect with Gen Zs and so we worked with 3 young talented artists to help us do just that through creative expression. This was such a fun campaign to be a part of. Many thanks to the whole team that was part of championing this creativity. 🥣🧡

Ashley Sawchuk Sarah Atteck Jenny Chiasson Ruth-Anne Hunsberger Mike Edgell Jayne Kitchen Holly Martin Emily Jones Isabel Lee Brad Dworkin 



https://lnkd.in/giUb98Cy

It’s not often you get to help reinvigorate a brand with so much brand love that consumers still quote our commercials today. This campaign was an opportunity to modernize Oatmeal Crisp while staying true to its humourous roots. We wanted to remind families that sometimes we need to embrace the chaos because “Life’s better with a bit of Crunch”. 🐶 A big shout out to the amazing talented team who helped bring this campaign to life! 

https://lnkd.in/g7Jh68py

Jenny Chiasson Ruth-Anne Hunsberger Anushka Gilani Scott Breckles Patrick Michaud Steven Morie Jessica Fecteau Patrick Levno Marc Angelini Nicolas Girault Alannah Virtanen Eloïse Bissonnette Juliana Diaz

One of the many reasons why I was so excited to work at General Mills Canada was their Olympic (25 years!) and now Paralympic partnership. Little did I know I would be trusted to lead our Paris 2024 Olympic and Paralympic athlete packaging design and help launch our new campaign through our brand platform Cherish the Good. A campaign that is aimed at reminding us that goodness goes beyond medals and sometimes it’s the small moments of good that nourish us the most.

The campaign includes a :30 TVC rooted in family connections and our Cheerios Summer Games! Inspired by our incredible athletes; Damian Warner, Cindy Ouellet, Maggie MacNeil, Caeli McKay and Aaron Brown we created family-friendly social challenges where Canadians can host their own games right in their backyard.

A huge shout out to the whole team who worked on this; including our talented partner agencies Cossette NARRATIVE PR Mindshare so.da Mark Graham  

Our partners at the COC & CPC Luke Turner Kristy Walton Rachael Pinnock

Our GM cross functional team including our incredible Cheerios BU team Ruth-Anne Hunsberger Anushka Gilani @Mariam Hassan

Finally a huge thank you to our Brand Experience Dream Team Shelly Bruch Ashley Sawchuk Jenny Chiasson for the opportunity to work alongside you talented ladies on this incredible campaign. 

We cannot wait to cheer on our athletes this summer! Go Canada!


https://lnkd.in/gpRrZvKn

https://lnkd.in/g4AH_rUA

recommendationsReceived.name: Mike Larrañaga
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Marketing Specialist at Mortenson
recommendationsReceived.context: Mike worked with Miranda on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I have had the pleasure of working with Miranda at multiple organizations and have always appreciated her exceptional balance of hard work, creativity, and fun as a colleague. Her innate talent for marketing is impressive and her commitment to growth fuels her continued success. 

Miranda is an exceptional team mate - always willing to lend a hand and find a path forward. Ideation and creativity come naturally to Miranda - this is apparent in her ability to bring new ideas to the table with ease and precision. My favorite part about working with Miranda is her ability to knuckle down on projects while maintaining a fun, light-hearted, can-do attitude. Her skill set is vast which makes her a great team mate to collaborate with and learn from. From my observations of Miranda as a leader, she is very detailed, planful, supportive, and kind. Overall - Miranda is amazing to work with and a very skilled, highly dedicated marketer.

recommendationsReceived.name: Jackie Augustin
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Marketing Associate | I deliver on the B2B Foodservice side of the business related to Pizza!
recommendationsReceived.description:
I have had the pleasure of working along side Miranda and can tell you she is one of my most memorable co-workers. During our time together, Miranda displayed great talents and leadership when it came to managing her projects within the Digital Marketing umbrella at DB. At times she managed many hats, highly conscientious in her planning efforts, follow-through and organization of said projects. She always found or delivered a proactive solution in highly stressful situations, which resulted in positive praises from leadership, greater user experiences for clients and increased loyalty from her colleagues.

She has always treated others with compassion, respect and professionalism and it gives me great pleasure in writing her this recommendation and hope our paths cross again in the future! I am always available for reference conversations - so just message me :)

recommendationsReceived.name: Joel B.
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Gone Riding @ retired and all that . . .
recommendationsReceived.context: Joel managed Miranda directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Miranda worked with High Monkey while attending college full-time. Miranda helped to create new marketing materials, wrote a huge number of client case studies, and totally owned High Monkey's social media outreach.  During an overhaul and redesign of the High Monkey website, Miranda did extensive website Accessibility compliance testing, created content management documentation, and helped keep content contributors on the High Monkey team on track.  Miranda played a big part in planning and hosting one of High Monkey's largest and most successful Live Events. Miranda one of those rare people managers hope to hire at least once in their careers . . . my job was to get out of her way and let her do great work.

recommendationsReceived.name: Melissa Boyles-Howieson
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Strategic Marketing Leader | Building Pipelines, Driving Growth | Sales-Driven, Data-Informed Approach
recommendationsReceived.context: Melissa worked with Miranda on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Miranda’s can-do attitude is infectious! In my time working with her at Direct Benefits, I have seen her tackle work hurdles with an admirable amount of self-directed urgency, tact, and thoughtfulness. It was a pleasure to work with someone who to always over-delivered, outperformed, and followed through.

recommendations.name: Jay Kallman
recommendations.subtitle: Restoring confidence in marketing & GTM teams | Fractional Marketing Leader | B2B SaaS, Platform & Partner-Led Growth | Franchise Enablement | Team Development
recommendations.context: Miranda reported directly to Jay
recommendations.description: Jay is a phenomenal leader with incredible skills building and developing teams that rally and produce high-quality output. On top of that, her strategic marketing skills and know-how are unmatched - She thinks holistically about demand generation and go-to-market strategy, asks all the 'right' questions, and consistently delivers exceptional results. Working with (and in my case, for!) Jay is both inspiring and enjoyable; She brings a contagious energy, genuine kindness, creativity, and love to everything she does!

recommendations.name: Rachel Auer
recommendations.subtitle: Product Marketing Leader | B2B Technology | Sales Enablement | Value Positioning | Product & Feature Launches | Customer Advocacy
recommendations.context: Miranda worked with Rachel on the same team
recommendations.description:
Rachel is a true product marketing genius! As Delaget's first-ever product marketer, she set the standard and established a robust foundation for product marketing at the company. Rachel introduced new ways of thinking to our marketing team, led strategic initiatives, and implemented a rock-solid framework for product launches.

Working with Rachel has been an absolute delight, and I would jump at the chance to collaborate with her again. She’s a team player, excels at collaboration, and consistently goes above and beyond to deliver exceptional results. Not only is she an outstanding professional, but she’s also a fantastic coworker and human being. I cannot recommend her highly enough!

recommendations.name: Sally Plampton
recommendations.subtitle: Creative Designer | Brand Strategist | Storyteller | Problem-Solver
recommendations.context: Miranda managed Sally directly
recommendations.description:
Sally isn’t “just” a Graphic Designer — she’s a powerhouse marketing strategist and an essential contributor to any team lucky enough to work with her. Her Graphic Design expertise is nothing short of remarkable: in my experience working with her, feedback has included praise like, “The best pitch deck I’ve ever seen,” and she’s been called the “MVP” of the Go-To-Market team more times than I can count. From visionary creative ideation and brand development to flawless technical execution, Sally delivers exceptional results every single time.

Her tireless work ethic, unshakable positivity, and commitment to personal and professional growth make her truly invaluable. Sally actively seeks out feedback and fosters meaningful connections, constantly refining her skills while uplifting those around her. She has an incredible knack for anticipating the needs of her team and always goes above and beyond to exceed expectations. Her unique ability to blend creative brilliance with strategic insight sets her apart as a standout professional and an indispensable team player.

recommendations.name: Mike Larrañaga
recommendations.subtitle: Marketing Specialist at Mortenson
recommendations.context: Miranda was senior to Mike but didn't manage Mike directly
recommendations.description:
Mike’s work ethic and meticulous attention to detail stand out in every project he undertakes. His ability and natural talents as a marketer and professional are further enhanced by his knowledge of technology and capacity to learn, which enable him to navigate and leverage new tools and processes effortlessly. 

Mike is disciplined, organized, and an exemplary team player. His interpersonal skills are outstanding, fostering an environment of collaboration and support. He has a can-do attitude, provides insightful feedback and advice whenever needed, and is a joy to work with!

experiences.title: Human Resource Coordinator
experiences.jobDescription: In my role, I created and maintained reports using Oracle HRIS, ensuring data accuracy, compliance, and providing technical guidance to staff. updates.postText:
This week, the Progresso soup team was recognized with EIGHT Cannes Lions shortlists and TWO wins for our work on Soup Drops - Gold in PR and Silver in Brand Experience & Activation. To say this is a career highlight is a vast understatement. Our little challenger soup brand made headlines and newsfeeds everywhere and was paid the ultimate compliment by being honored on the world stage.

I am, however, fully cognizant that this honor was built on the backs of many hard working and wonderful people. To our Edelman team (Laura K. Fries, Marisa Biaggi, Erin Delahanty, Courtney Miller, Ryan VandenBosch, Julia Matthews, Katie Stemler Ashbrook, Arielle Lyons, Ali Barzyk and MANY OTHERS), THANK YOU for bringing this idea forward, helping to execute so flawlessly and gently nudging us when we needed it. Thank you Knechtel Inc. for your candy expertise (never thought I would say that about soup.) To our friends at Promotion Management Center Inc. (PMC) (Holdyn Erickson) thank you for your diligence in managing such a viral campaign. To The Social Lights, thank you for your thoughtful approach to paid boosting to ensure all eyes were on us.

Internally at General Mills, THANK YOU to Maria Carolina Comings and Michael Tensley for championing such an innovative (read: risky) idea. Kelly Thompson, Kenzi Clark and John Sijord, thank you for being such collaborative cross-functional partners and answering all of my "What would need to be true if...?" questions. Andrea Williamson, thank you for being my partner in crime and sounding board to get this wild idea off the ground and into the hands of consumers. Finally, to Susan Corrigan Pitt and Shannon Heine, thank you for blocking, tackling and empowering me to take the lead on what will be a campaign I can't stop talking about for years to come. I am so appreciative of your mentorship and leadership, and credit this career high to you.

Suck on that!
Look ma, no spoon! I am SO excited to launch this wacky and wonderful promotion - a true labor of love along with our teams at Edelman, The Social Lights, PMC and many others.

Ecstatic to announce Yoplait's powerful partnership with Girls Inc., launching today!

For years, Yoplait products have strengthened women and girls by being a good source of calcium and building strong bones. With teen girls experiencing an alarming drop in self-confidence, Yoplait is now focused on building up more than just physical strength by also supporting the emotional and mental wellness of young girls.

In partnership with Girls Inc., Yoplait is furthering their message of female empowerment and helping women and girls everywhere to Open Up and Be Bold by sharing their strengths. Because by embarking on the simple, bold act of saying the qualities you love about yourself out loud, you can build up your own confidence! 

Excited to be a part of this brand taking a stance in how girls view themselves. Share your strengths @yoplaitusa.

I’m a strategic, data-driven marketer with a passion for building and evolving brands. I have a proven track record of success developing, executing, and optimizing best-in-class multi-channel marketing campaigns to drive business goals for global corporations such as Penguin Random House and General Mills. With a focus in digital marketing and integrated marketing communications, I empower brands to stay relevant, authentic, and ahead of the curve. 

Contact me at SarahAEngelmann@gmail.com

SPECIALITIES: digital marketing strategy, content strategy, brand strategy, data strategy, site strategy, agency management, production, partnership, influencer marketing, copywriting, SEO, SEM, paid social, organic social, email marketing, programmatic, Google AdWords, Google Analytics, integrated marketing communications, omnichannel campaigns, brand marketing

experiences.title: Sr. Planner, Brand Experience- Pillsbury
experiences.jobDescription:
• Lead strategy across Pillsbury’s inbound and outbound digital channels (paid and organic social, SEO, SEM, email, site) to deliver fully stacked omnichannel marketing campaigns; increased Pillsbury’s owned media response index by 20% and volume contribution YoY despite decrease in investment. 

• Implement paid media strategy across paid search, social, and programmatic to increase efficiency of traffic driving and acquisition campaigns, pivoting objectives and deploying A/B tests to optimize budget spend against goals

• Lead management of digital agency partners — including scoping, briefing, and day-to-day management — to drive collaborative relationships and ensure efficient workflows among internal and external teams 

• Pioneered new digital content tagging and reporting structure to optimize digital marketing  insight share-out across departments, ensuring marketing campaigns across channels are optimized based on consumer data. Optimizations led to a 14.5% increase in organic Facebook KPI within first month, and increased efficacy in paid media campaigns

• Craft clear, actionable briefs and work closely with external production partners to develop assets across channels (digital, social, linear TV, print, and more) that resonate with Pillsbury’s strategic target and reinforce brand identity 

• Evolved Pillsbury’s visual identity by developing a brand photo/video guide that drives consistency in creative assets across channels and increases brand recognition among consumers

• Optimize budget spend in partnership activations by monetizing content to leverage as trade to secure incremental reach, creating and pitching media proposals to external clients and working cross-functionally to deliver campaigns that are aligned to Pillsbury and partner brand goals

• Collaborate with corporate crisis communications team to monitor international situation involving Pillsbury, proactively pausing or pivoting messaging to minimize negative brand perception

recommendationsReceived.name: Lauren Weber
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Executive Director at Penguin Random House | Brand Development Expert | Social Media Strategist | Digital Marketer | Campaign Manager | Creative Storyteller and Content Creator
recommendationsReceived.context: Lauren worked with Sarah on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Sarah is an incredible marketer who I was lucky enough to work alongside for 5 years. Working with Sarah pushed me to be better in my own work because she set the bar so high. While together at Penguin Random House, I saw firsthand how she fused innovation and creativity into every one of her campaigns. She is organized and has an analytical eye for data that makes it easy to track and learn from the effectiveness of her strategies. She was an amazing colleague to brainstorm ideas with, always giving her well thought out and honest opinion that made every project ideas even better. I can't recommend Sarah highly enough!

recommendationsReceived.name: Judy Jacoby
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Vice President, DIrector Creative Marketing and Advertising Services, Knopf Doubleday
recommendationsReceived.context: Judy worked with Sarah on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: I worked with Sarah for five years in the Doubleday Marketing Department.  She is brilliant in many areas:  social media management, marketing strategy, creative promotion, and stellar writing skills.  Her Instagram posts for Doubleday never ceased to amaze me.  She wrote about books that I had already read in such a unique and persuasive way, that I wanted to read them all over again.  It's a unique talent, and I hope that she will be able to continue to exercise this special creative muscle in her next job.  In addition to her creative skills, Sarah is also an organized multi-tasker with a tireless work ethic.  Sarah gets in there and gets it done beautifully every time.  I am very, very jealous of the team that gets to work with her next.  I highly recommend her without reservation.

recommendationsReceived.name: Lisa Gudding
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: President @ Ipsos
recommendationsReceived.description: Sarah is a rising star who is considering a shift from publishing to marketing/insights in a new industry. Ask her about the debut fiction novels she turned into New York Times bestsellers, or her work on the recent marketing campaign for The Testaments (Margaret Atwood's sequel to The Handmaid's Tale). Her digital marketing skills and passion will translate well to so many arenas. Super-smart, fun to be around, always game for a new challenge--all strong traits she possesses. Any team would be fortunate to count her as a member. I highly recommend Sarah.

recommendationsReceived.name: Michael Goldsmith
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: VP Senior Publicity Director, Doubleday at Penguin Random House
recommendationsReceived.context: Michael worked with Sarah but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: Sarah and I have been colleagues at Penguin Random House for nearly five years and in that time have collaborated on promotional campaigns to support the publication of many high profile books on the Doubleday list. As a publicist, I've had fantastic firsthand exposure to Sarah's diligent work ethic and boundless creative energy, which have made her an invaluable asset on our marketing team and a wonderful partner on title campaigns. She brings a consistently fresh perspective to all of her projects, from debut novels to branded house authors, and is always prepared to contribute her expertise to our broader publishing lists beyond her own personal responsibilities. It's a pleasure to have her voice represent our imprint identity on key social media platforms and it's been a thrill to watch Doubleday's brand grow and evolve under her stewardship.

recommendationsReceived.name: Erin Kottke
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: career & college readiness
recommendationsReceived.context: Erin managed Sarah directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Sarah is the absolute best! I was amazed by how quickly she dove into the job and took control over all of her tasks. No task is too big or too small--and whatever it is, she does it quickly, accurately, and with a positive attitude. She made a huge impact during her time here, and helped the marketing department to become more efficient and productive. We miss her!

recommendationsReceived.name: Laura Pearle
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Academy Archivist
recommendationsReceived.context: Laura was senior to Sarah but didn't manage Sarah directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
During my time with The Center for Fiction as their Head Librarian, I worked with Sarah on the Books for NYC Schools project (10,000 or so books collected and loosely categorized by a high school student, donated to the CFF for distribution to 40+ schools in NYC for classroom and all-school libraries).  

Sarah's ability to quickly identify what was and was not acceptable as a donation, both in terms of subject matter and condition of the books, was very helpful in the processing of the books.  She recognized that the very loose sorting done by the donor was not going to work well and showed great judgement in how to tackle separating middle grade from high school materials.  She also showed sensitivity to the issues of overtly political and religious screeds arriving in a school and how that might be problematic.   When Sarah had questions about a book or genre, she asked for guidance and was able to extrapolate from that to other books being sorted.

Her ability to manage a project this size while working only two days a week was impressive.  I have no reservations recommending her work to any book-related employer.

recommendationsReceived.name: Christine Onorati
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Licensed Clinical Social Worker and Owner of WORD Bookstores
recommendationsReceived.context: Christine managed Sarah directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Though Sarah was not with us very long, she proved to be a competent, assertive, fast learner and was absolutely wonderful with customers. She had customer service perfected and knew how to handle many different situations, and she was a fantastic bookseller. She enjoyed recommending books to customers and it showed; her enthusiasm was infectious. Everyone loved working with Sarah, and I believe she'd be a wonderful asset to any business.

recommendationsReceived.name: Becky Redetzke Field
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Victim Witness Advocate at Ramsey County
recommendationsReceived.context: Becky managed Sarah directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Sarah is able to maintain a level head while dealing with all sorts of crises, a must for the ever-changing work of an advocate.  She is able to connect with survivors while also maintaining professionalism and able to think on her feet to find great resources and solutions.

recommendationsReceived.name: Jerie Smith
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Retired Volunteer Coordinator The Aurora Center at University of MN
recommendationsReceived.context: Sarah was Jerie's client
recommendationsReceived.description: I was Sarah's supervisor as she lived out a commitment as an help-line advocate fielding calls on a 24-hour basis in regards to sexual assault, relationship violence and stalking. Her responsibility was to answer calls and if necessary accompany a client to the hospital, enable a police report and/or provide others necessary resources. The Aurora Center is part of student services at the U of MN and works with students, faculty and staff primarily. The 40 hour training involved certified Sarah as a sexual assault counselor in the State of MN. Sarah performed her volunteer duties well and appropriately. During her time with us, she also applied for and was accepted to coordinate a variety of activities for Domestic Violence Awareness Month (October) which she did with creativity and skill.  As with many good volunteers in a University environment, they graduate and I was sorry to see her leave

recommendations.name: Lauren Weber
recommendations.subtitle: Executive Director at Penguin Random House | Brand Development Expert | Social Media Strategist | Digital Marketer | Campaign Manager | Creative Storyteller and Content Creator
recommendations.context: Lauren was senior to Sarah but didn't manage Sarah directly
recommendations.description: Without a doubt, Lauren is someone you want on your team. She has the innate creativity to come up with marketing plans no one’s ever thought of before, and the strategic chops to executive them flawlessly. Lauren is well-known within the Knopf Doubleday Group for her extreme attention to detail and her can-do attitude—she’s someone people naturally turn to to solve a problem. It’s clear to everyone that Lauren will say yes to any new challenge. If she doesn’t have the answer, she’ll find it. As an excellent mentor and collaborative colleague, Lauren knows how to bring out the best in everyone. Anyone who’s had the pleasure of working with Lauren—as a colleague or a client—knows she is an asset to any team.

recommendationsReceived.context: Greta managed Kaylee directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
I was honored to have Kaylee join my team. She is one of those rare employees who isn't afraid of any task, pushes herself to always find the best solution to a problem, and reliably delivers over and over again.

I was thoroughly impressed with how quickly she on-boarded, and was able to navigate the complex organization of General Mills. She was able to find a way to save the brand team over $1 million by finding a creative sampling solution within the first three months of her tenure--an incredible feat. 

Kaylee has a creative spirit and is energized by bringing ideas forward, and helping the team uncover ideas. She regularly brought inspiration to the team and mined external perspective/examples to generate new campaign surround ideas. 

Kaylee handled a broad range of projects, proving that she is incredibly flexible within her role, and has astounding capacity. She takes great accountability for her work, and delivers results against her projects. Kaylee is not afraid to roll up her sleeves and go beyond the specific task at hand to deliver the best idea or result for her brands.

Kaylee came to I Imagine Studio as a marketing intern and was promoted to Marketing Coordinator. Her help and expertise in areas such as social media strategy and market research have been invaluable to both I Imagine’s clients and the agency itself.

Kaylee has a number of strengths that make her an indispensable team member. She is a very positive person with a lot of energy and motivation. She is also very passionate about the online marketing world and is constantly seeking new information, which she proactively applies to her projects. In addition, she has been a crucial asset to our business development efforts, attending events/meetings in order to build relationships with new clients. Kaylee learned very quickly how to conduct market research, create competitive analyses, and develop recommendations for the agency clients. Kaylee is a strong presenter who is able to build a relationship with potential clients.

If given the opportunity, I would love to work with Kaylee again. I am sure she has a very bright future ahead of her and look forward to seeing what she accomplishes.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAI2nXQB.jobTitle: Freelance Marketing & Media Consulting | Minneapolis, MN | Present
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAI2nXQB.about:
I’m a strategic marketing and media leader who helps brands and organizations turn business goals into clear, effective marketing and media strategies that drive growth.

After building my career across both agency and client-side environments, working on global, billion-dollar brands and multi-million-dollar marketing investments, I launched my consulting practice to help companies access senior-level media and marketing leadership when they need it most — whether as a strategic partner, fractional leader, or an extension of an existing team.

My expertise sits at the intersection of brand strategy, media strategy, and execution, ensuring plans are not only smart on paper but operationally cohesive across creative, media, retail, and analytics teams.

How I Help Organizations:

- Develop integrated media strategies across planning, buying, execution, and optimization

- Align brand, creative, media, and retail teams to create cohesive go-to-market plans

- Build and strengthen agency and partner relationships

- Translate performance data into action using campaign reporting, analytics, and MMM insights

- Provide fractional marketing or media leadership during growth, transitions, or resource gaps

- Bring clarity, structure, and momentum to complex marketing initiatives

I’m known for being a collaborative leader, strategic thinker, and strong relationship builder who can quickly step into organizations, understand business challenges, and move work forward.

I enjoy partnering with large, mid-size, and growing businesses looking to elevate their marketing effectiveness, scale media investment responsibly, or bring senior-level strategy to their teams.

📩 Open to consulting, freelance, and fractional leadership opportunities. I am excited to partner with you, let's chat! Email me: Kherrstrategy@gmail.com

experiences.title: Freelance Marketing & Media Consulting | Minneapolis, MN | Present
experiences.jobDescription:
Strategic marketing and media consultant helping brands, agencies, and growing businesses develop smarter, more effective marketing strategies. I partner with organizations as a fractional leader or embedded team extension, bringing deep expertise in media strategy, brand growth, and cross-channel campaign execution.

Key Contributions & Capabilities

- Develop and lead integrated media strategies across paid, owned, and earned channels, including media planning, buying, optimization, and campaign execution.

- Partner with brands and leadership teams to shape holistic marketing strategies, aligning creative, media, retail, and digital teams to ensure cohesive brand storytelling and effective go-to-market execution.

- Build and manage strategic partnerships with agencies, media platforms, influencers, and industry partners to maximize reach, performance, and brand impact.

- Translate complex performance data into actionable strategy using marketing analytics, campaign reporting, and Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM) to guide investment decisions and drive business outcomes.

- Lead campaign planning and execution across multiple channels, ensuring alignment between brand strategy, media investment, and retail or commerce priorities.

- Provide fractional marketing leadership or short-term support during times of growth, transition, or increased business need—serving as a strategic extension of internal teams.

*Known for building strong relationships, leading cross-functional collaboration, and developing clear strategic direction that drives measurable marketing and business results.

updates.postText: As I come to my two-year anniversary at General Mills, like an aged orator looking back over their life, I've been looking back on what will be fifteen years in Marketing and Communications. And, because the last time I wrote something entitled '5 things...' I got over 7,000 views, I thought sod it, five things it is again. So here goes. Nobody asked for it, but here are '5 things I've learnt about brand building, the brand experience edition'. As the phrase goes, I am one of those weirdos who has 'completed media', having worked Media Owner - Bauer Media Group - Media Agency - Mindshare UK - and now, having gone client side with responsibilities over, yes, Media (among other things) at the aforementioned General Mills. And from that mix of experience, I'm going to mark #1 on this list as the dreaded 'P Word': 1) Believing in....Purpose Now, I'm sorry, I'm "that guy", I've said it but it's true, and not in the way you think it. In my agency days I'd hear the word 'Purpose' bounded about in strategy meetings, or by clients terrified that their brand didn't have a purpose in the life of their target consumer, or moreover no idea how to bring it to life. Some were obvious by virtue of their innate utility, some were boundless in their lofty ambitions, and of course, some were downright ridiculous Marketing mumbo-jumbo, usually made up by us the hapless keen-to-impress agency. Sorry about that. But, seeing it from the benefit of being in teams who have to create the content used to unlock any idealised purpose, (and any Media Owner creative will sympathise) and then coming to the dark-cough-client side and working at identifying and articulating my very own purposes (probably not a word, but I'm okay with it), I've realised that it really is as crucial as they say in brand building. So yes, I think we should believe in purpose. Not the flower-puffery of many a BBC modern day 'I work in Marketing' drama, but in the simple task of speaking to consumers, finding out why products like yours add value to their lives, and then finding a way to build your brand off of it. When you put it this way, the dreaded 'P word' loses some of its illusion and mysticism and becomes incredibly powerful and, my all time favourite trait in anything - actionable. In recent years, identifying a simple, actionable purpose, without the veneer of marketing vanity, has informed brand, product, communications, CSR, and likely will do beyond. I'm oddly enjoying all this purpose malarky. If it isn't 'Live. Love. Laugh', is it okay? 2) Back yourself Like many of my generation, you would have moved jobs every 2-4 years, at least had a meaningful shift in your role if you've stayed uncharacteristically loyal to one employer. Full transparency, I have a 4 year average. The debate about waifish 30-somethings aside, you will well know the challenges you're faced with staying true to not only who you are, but the value you bring. In my experience, every time you make a step change in your career you have to rightly justify your place in the new team, but sadly you will likely have to justify your talents, expertise, experience, knowledge bank, intuitions and statements of fact again and again. It can be a frustrating experience but make sure you take a pause and check back in with what will undoubtedly be a rich tapestry of experience people should wake up to the value of in you. If you do this that network of experience and perspectives is invaluable to brands. Not just because brands live and die by being fuelled by provocation and ideas, but also because as humans stuck in the same state, we stagnate ever so little. And stagnation leads to decay. And no consumer is buying decay (unless you're that Burger King ad that got everyone excited. Not to enter in to that vicious debate around it's effectiveness) If not, my life advice is to grab a beer (many) and get your greatest work-mates together for an epic putting-the-world-to-rights session down the pub Kelly Benjko Nicola Borradaile Steve Proud are all too aware of the value I place on the latter. Try it. They even do non-alcoholic beer now, apparently. Looks like a laugh 3. Open up to connection Let's not get too emotional. I'm one of those people with alarmingly few outward emotions, often misread as being constantly annoyed...(or am I?) but I can safely say I have managed to work with some truly amazing people. Some taught me the true meaning of what great work looks like, with an almost religious attention to detail and excellence. These people are often crucified for an almost Anna-Wintouristic team dynamic but I am all here for it. Trust me, if you don't know what 'good' looks like, you're going to be the target of all the water-cooler moments (what is the Teams version of a water cooler moment?) and you really don't want that. And if you don't know, ask your manager. And if they don't know...leave. Quick. Others taught me that our jobs are hyper-integrated. The joy of getting a tight group round a table, the classic media-wa**er Sharpie and A3 paper moment, and working out who can bring what to the table to make ideas and executions great. Or more often, how to dig yourselves out of whatever shitshow any particular Monday, Thursday, Tuesday, Wednesday brings you. Never Fridays, Fridays should be spent in the pub 'brainstorming'. Being able to identify those who are part of those 'greats' is the ticket to an accelerated and more importantly, satisfying career in building brands. And then others taught me we're not saving lives here. Or rather, I'm not. Perhaps one of you handful of readers actually do, in which case, ignore me now and always, I'm full of rubbish. By this I mean, never take yourself too seriously. I'm sure there are many out there who say "but we're a serious business", and yes, you are. But the reality is, to quote Mark Ritson et al, nobody really cares about Marketing(eers), therefore, lighten up, open up, and have some fun. Lightening up will liberate you to think of the bigger picture. Being more open in the work you do will open you up to new and better ideas. Having fun will give you more reasons to wake up and tackle the day with excitement and curiosity. If you can embrace that connection with people, you will not only be better at your job today, but forever more. And like me, constantly pulling on the same group of inspiring 'greats' that you discovered on your career journey. With any luck, some of those will become great friends. The aforementioned trio can satisfy themselves in being in that group along with people I've since forgotten were 'work mates' to begin with Suzie Lawler Sarah Clarke James Wilson Lucy Brinson Lucy-Jane Walker As a Brand Builder, these connections are your life-blood. They keep you close to the things happening across the industry, they keep you grounded to what it takes to deliver plans and ideas, and in an inevitable pickle you can draw upon those same people who saved your bacon in previous roles to offer impartial support and left-field solutions to the problems you're facing. Remember, brand building is a team exercise. Not just the team you're in, but your wider stakeholders, agencies, consumers, insight partners, customers, friends down the pub... The painting of ones hands is optional 4. If you can edit, you get it This is a rich statement from someone who has spent this much time warbling on about their own unsubstantiated opinions on brand building, but hey, this is my LinkedIn article and I'm indulging. Anyone who has been within 5 feet of a brand strategy will know what I mean here. There are phrases, frameworks, consumer profiles, 4Ps, positioning and more and you'll need to do all of that in the fewest, most crowd-pleasing, most motivating, most all-applicable phrases possible. If you can edit, you've won. Much of my time is now spent editing. Editing strategy, editing copy. And to be honest, I learnt that all in my time in Radio nearly a decade ago. Having to write advertising copy in ten or thirty seconds is a surprisingly heroic undertaking and that's before you even consider some of the challenging content you're asked to muster up (sharp memories flocking back of a certain government campaign Kathleen Moroney). If anyone has ever said to you, less is more, they were right. And if that person is your Marketing Director, they mean it. Learn to edit. Or, as Coco Chanel put it - always look in the mirror before you leave the house, and take something off. The chainsaw is real. Use it. But if you like Chanel, saw elegantly. 5. Empower your teams Point #2 on this brain-dump of my inner machinations was not meant to mean you know everything. Marketing Teams are often a central cog to teams working across every area of brands. ITQ, Supply Chain, Manufacturing, Sourcing, Creative, and more. My advice is you get the hell out of their way and let them find the confidence to explore and navigate the best solutions for the brands you work on or support. Your job is to bring all that magic together in to something cohesive in the real world to meet real consumer needs. If you're lucky enough to have floated to the top of the pile in that team, your role is to always be on hand to support, provide solutions to particularly sticky problems, deliver the guardrails and ultimate expectations, definitely to define the 'what good looks like'. But in my experience, amazing people come from amazing team structures and environments. I'll be ever-grateful to Darren Coomansingh for instilling this in me upon my first ever job in Media, the enigmatic phrase "don't F*&K it up" my constant north start ever since. That is drawn in to tight focus when you deal with agency partners. The luxury and quite frankly, the joy of working with agencies should be a liberating experience. As the client your soul interest should be how you can unlock their potential, their interest, their excitement, their value, through empowerment. It isn't, contrary to belief, about getting their costs down to the bare bones. I'm hugely lucky to work with the teams at VCCP but you'd have to ask them if I practice what I preach. There's Teams, and then there's Teams. Which you choose to engage with more, is your choice. Have to do the disclaimer part. All my inane opinions are, quite obviously, my own. And of course, here come the hashtags. #brandbuilding #branding #marketing #brandexperience
updates.subTitle: 5 things I've learnt about brand building, brand experience edition
updates.title: 5 things I've learnt about brand building, brand experience edition

updates.postText:
It's been over a year but I couldn't be happier to share our latest campaign for Nature Valley: 'Get Equipped For Life'. What a hugely exciting journey for the brand but also me personally, kicking off my career with General Mills in style! 

Massive thank you to our partners VCCP who landed a great insight that delivered an ownable creative strategy and executional platform. Special thanks to Sophie Binns Ed Taylor Holly Stockley Sean Kelly Ross Neil Josh Clarke Daisy Elsden Suki Fraser Roly Darby and the whole production team.

Also a great internal partnership across business functions with Cameron Hardacre Jordan Smith Anna Diehl Capucine PORCHER LIGIER Magdalena Kilgour-Miller Patrick Levno 
....

 It's landed! Nature Valley – the world’s No.1 Cereal Bar – has relaunched the brand internationally to help happily hectic families better take on their day and become an essential in the family snack cupboard. The ‘Get Equipped For Life’ campaign scored an impressive System1 5* exceptional rating with families. Each spot tackles a typical happily hectic moment in the life of busy families: the weekend swim trip, the dog walk, and the morning scooter trip to the park, and aligns that to key platforms and benefits within the range. Assets include a new TVC, Key Visuals and a host of digital and social assets aligned to key consumption occasions. 
...

#advertising #creativejourney #brandbuilding #marketingcampaign
