recommendationsReceived.name: Alicia Afza
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Brand Management Professional with 12 years experience in FMCG
recommendationsReceived.context: Alicia worked with Andrew but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: Drew is an absolute joy to work with. An inspired and inspiring leader, always ready to roll up his sleeve and come up with new (always great) ideas. I’m impressed by his level of creativity and problem solving, and the positivity he brings to all situations. I’m fortunate enough to work very closely with Drew and I would go as far as saying he’s my workplace soulmate! I love his energy, passion and enthusiasm and also his ability to think strategically and with a clear vision. I can’t commend him more for the impeccable work he recently done on one of our brands, completely rehauling the way we show up and adding so much relevance to what we mean to consumers. I’m proud to work with him and can honestly I have learned SO much from Drew and continue to learn from him. I could not dream of a better Global Brand Experience leader to collabore with, and consider myself super lucky to be able to both grow and have fun in the process thanks to him. Drew is a charismatic and creative leader and he would make any team much, much better.

recommendationsReceived.name: Loren Eley
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Client Service, Account Management, Strategy, Project Management
recommendationsReceived.description: I've worked with Drew both as a colleague and as an agency partner. He is super talented, intelligent, tenacious and he works with media owners to create incredible partnerships for his clients. I hope to work with him again!

recommendationsReceived.name: Liz Cunningham
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Forward Slash Films
recommendationsReceived.context: Liz managed Andrew directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
Drew is brave, creative and innovative and he has a well-rounded, strong understanding of both clients and audiences.  
He presents with fantastic confidence and authority, and is a strong leader of teams and projects.
He is always keen to explore new ways of doing things, and keen to apply new tech and media opportunities to campaigns.  
There is never a dull moment working with Drew - he is extremely well liked by clients and colleagues. 
His drive and ambition is relentless, and he's sure to bring success to many clients, campaigns and teams throughout his career.

recommendationsReceived.name: Lucy Brinson
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Group Client Lead
recommendationsReceived.context: Lucy was senior to Andrew but didn't manage Andrew directly
recommendationsReceived.description: I have found Andrew to be a thoroughly professional, forward thinking and first rate commercial programmer. His "can do"/ solution led attitude is always refreshing especially in the high pressure/quick turnaround environment in which we often find ourselves.

recommendationsReceived.name: Lillian Betty
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Corporate Partnerships, Content Marketing,
recommendationsReceived.context: Lillian was senior to Andrew but didn't manage Andrew directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Andrew is highly proficient; he demonstrates a great understanding of audience and brand and is able to channel his knowledge into creative and original ideas. He is always professional, a terrific colleague who is enjoyable to work with.

recommendationsReceived.name: Emily Stala
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: UK Head of Sales
recommendationsReceived.context: Emily worked with Andrew but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: Andrew is a dedicated and passionate Commercial Programmer. He has been amazing to work with over the past year and I always feel confident in his ability and presenting skills when introducing him to my clients. His Power Point presentations are always immaculate and he uses great insight and research in all his work.

recommendationsReceived.name: James Rowland
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Head of Performance Marketing at Chestertons - CIM Marketer of the Year Winner
recommendationsReceived.context: James worked with Andrew on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Andrew was the perfect professional, very creative in his role, always the idea generator in a brainstorming session, was a pleasure to work with him.

recommendationsReceived.name: Kat Pouwer
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Head of Agency Partnerships EMEA at Adthena
recommendationsReceived.context: Kat managed Andrew directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
Andrew has a true passion for all things online and PR related and throughout his time at lowcostholidays.com this was very clear. He approaches tasks with a can-do approach and constantly brings fresh ideas to the table and does this all with a smile on his face.

A primary objective within Andrew's role was to grow and develop our social media strategy and every month he achieved his targets - sometimes fairly ambitious targets too - and often smashed them.

Andrew conducts himself in a professional manner and tackles new challenges head on - certainly a great addition to any team!

recommendationsReceived.name: Anna Morton (nee Paynton)
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Primary School Teacher - Education, Marketing & PR Professional
recommendationsReceived.context: Anna managed Andrew directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Andrew is a great marketeer: flexible, hardworking, keen to get involved, good at generating ideas and copywriting and when he worked in our team he clearly understood our brands and audiences.  A great team member.

recommendations.name: Darren Coomansingh
recommendations.subtitle: Business Development | Creative Direction | Experienced Media Professional | Mental Health Advocate | Mentor | Trustee | Former Governor
recommendations.context: Andrew worked with Darren but they were at different companies
recommendations.description:
Darren will always stand out for me for his leadership style: open & clear, encouraging & supportive. But above all driven to achieve goals as effectively as possible, together as a team,  while attempting to enjoy the process at the same time.

I think this is why so many of his prior team members and clients are privileged enough to still call him a friend and mentor, and why so many of us have adopted much of his leadership example as we’ve gone on to build our own careers.

He was the first to bring the phrase “if they don’t win, we don’t win” to my vocabulary and it has been my North Star ever since. Knowing cross functional collaboration is at the heart of excellence, that there is a special cadence to agency, client and customer relationships that fuels success, and that people are at the heart of commercial progress.

Darren is a calm but deliberate leader, one who has shaped and forged the careers of so many who are now at the top of their game.

recommendations.name: Alicia Afza
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Brand Management Professional with 12 years experience in FMCG
recommendations.context: Andrew worked with Alicia on the same team
recommendations.description:
Alicia is my right hand woman at work. Always on hand to challenge the status quo, offer sharp minded thinking, present a consumer/market/business opportunity or challenge clearly and succinctly and work as a team to deliver amazing results.
Driven, curious, creative and agile, she thrives on high pressure, big output workstreams where bringing out the best is encouraged and expected.

recommendations.name: Ivan Lazarov
recommendations.subtitle: Commercial Events Director
recommendations.context: Andrew was Ivan's client
recommendations.description: Ivan is a real pleasure to work with, taking often complicated briefs and working them through to offer simple, impactful ideas that nail it every time. Rooted in candid consumer first thinking and always executed to the highest standard. A true team player who leads from the front

recommendations.name: Loren Eley
recommendations.subtitle: Client Service, Account Management, Strategy, Project Management
recommendations.description: Loren is one of those rare people who manages to hold both enthusiasm and positivity in everything she does, whatever is thrown her way. Her passion for people and creativity shines through in her relationships and work making her a joy to collaborate with.

recommendations.name: Dean Marks
recommendations.subtitle: Media, Marketing and Commercial professional; TV and Video strategy, planning and buying specialist; Masters in Sports Leadership.
recommendations.context: Andrew worked with Dean but on different teams
recommendations.description:
Working in Invention - Mindshare’s content partnerships team - Dean’s readiness to help explain, lead and deliver on any given project was invaluable. 

He has a wealth of knowledge but applies a huge amount of curiosity and intuitiveness to his role, making him a dream to work with. 

We shared some tough times but always found a solution, and award-winning ones at that! Ensuring clients were happy and our work was the best it could be.

recommendations.name: Liam Hannan
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Lead, Global Connections Strategy
recommendations.context: Andrew worked with Liam on the same team
recommendations.description:
Liam has always been a joy to work with, creating and leading strong strategies for a range of diverse clients through cross-team and cross-discipline  collaboration, always grounded is human, reasoned insights, that are easily communicated, understood and executed.

His personal approach has always fostered a great working relationship that benefits both day-to-day working practices as a team, and meeting ever evolving client expectations.

Strategy, and good strategy moreover, has increasingly become integral to everything we do as the world continues to change and the way we communicate continues to diversify. Liam is always on hand to encourage and support creativity and innovation that helps to bring to life a solid, central piece of strategic thinking. His flexibility allows teams to shine, while his well thought-out reasoning ensures ideas and projects stay on track, while everyone enjoys the process along the way.

recommendations.name: Liz Cunningham
recommendations.subtitle: Forward Slash Films
recommendations.context: Andrew reported directly to Liz
recommendations.description: Liz' passion for content is always evident in the work she does. Driving creative-led thinking on briefs to achieve real stand-out, cut-through and ingenuity. Always quick to adopt an understanding for new platforms and content opportunities, working with Liz means you never stand still. As a result, our team were able to build and create some truly exciting solutions for clients. All met with an unyielding ambition for making everything right for audiences to return clients the best engagement and ROI.

recommendations.name: Angela Soteriou
recommendations.subtitle: Partnerships I Branded Content I Leadership
recommendations.context: Andrew reported directly to Angela
recommendations.description: Angela is one of those people who will always go far. Her friendly, intelligent and solutions-first approach to work is a real asset in a team but upmost in a manager. She was able to guide a large fresh-faced-to-media PM team through some very tricky partnerships whilst always keeping spirits high, targets achievable and clients/suppliers happy with all our endeavours.A real pleasure to work with who leaves a big hole in the teams she moves on from.

recommendations.name: Marios Morris
recommendations.subtitle: Championing digital growth: Unlocking human potential and organisational agility.
recommendations.context: Andrew worked with Marios on the same team
recommendations.description:
Marios manages all our on-site content through a large network of Freelance writers. As the business expands Marios has showed initiative and excellent management skills, offering support across the Marketing team and delivering on creative solutions to problems on site, within social media and across content based Marketing strategies. 
Excellent team player - an asset to the team!

recommendationsReceived.name: Jim Pietruszynski
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Chief Executive Officer at Soulsight
recommendationsReceived.context: Jim worked with Licia on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
This letter is my personal recommendation for Licia Yanez. Until just recently, Licia has worked with us as an Account Manager. Licia is  consistently kind, an amazing team player and  has a passionate and dedicated work ethic.

Besides being a joy to work with, Licia is an excellent communicator. She can strategically manage and  present creative ideas in a seemingly effortless manner. Licia has successfully helped strengthen our brand and design strategy as well as build client relationships. During her tenure, she organized and managed multiple projects and  worked across many different consumer categories. Her work style and organization has been complimented by many of our clients.

Not only was she was an asset to our account team, Licia was also extraordinarily helpful in other areas of the company. She participated in the creative process, copywriting and actively helped research and audit materials for support in our process.

I highly recommend Licia for employment. She is a team player and would make a great asset to any organization. 

Sincerely,
James Pietruszynski
Partner // Creative Director
Soulsight®

recommendationsReceived.name: Erin Paul
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Strategy and Brand Design Consultant // Bridging the gap between insight and execution
recommendationsReceived.context: Erin managed Licia directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
I consider myself lucky to have the opportunity to write a recommendation for Licia. She has been an incredible asset to Soulsight, forging strong client relationships and showing great poise managing complex, stressful and fast-moving projects. She has excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, which help the client and agency teams develop a shared language and understanding, imperative in moving a project forward on time and on budget. Licia is a wonderful team leader as well, and her positive, "can-do" attitude make her an absolute pleasure to work with. Her design sensitivity is balanced by a strong business accumen which gives her the ability to manage high-profile design and innovation projects and confidently speak and present to a variety of clients—from brand to design managers to senior leadership. 

Even though she was was responsible for managing projects from the account side, Licia also contributed heavily to the strategic work needed for  many of the projects she managed and was also a valuable creative contributor—supporting the team on innovation ideation, nomenclature and copy writing.

Licia would be a tremendous addition to any team looking for a strong and capable person with an excellent and varied skill set and a super star personality!

recommendationsReceived.name: Traci Cox
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Marketing Communications Writer at Anthem, Inc.
recommendationsReceived.context: Traci worked with Licia on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Licia led numerous marketing jobs I was involved in. Her ability to write clear, concise and strategically sound briefs is definitely one of her many strengths. She can successfully guide and influence numerous business partners while dealing with multiple channels and complex financial products. She's a  wonderful meeting facilitator with excellent verbal and analytical ability. Because of Licia's dedication and ability to make every job a success I always felt confident going into presentations. I thinks it's safe to say that under Licia's leadership our team always left our clients 101% satisfied.

recommendationsReceived.name: Bob Saydlowski
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Retired Brand Identity Mgr / Creative Director / Art Director. Still Active Drummer!
recommendationsReceived.context: Bob worked with Licia on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Licia is one of the best Brand Managers I've ever worked with. She's extremely detail-oriented, totally buttoned up and very efficient.  And she's very smart too! When she's on a project with me, I can rest easy knowing she'll take care of business flawlessly. Licia will be a definite asset to any company she works for -- I'd give her a Five-Star recommendation, for sure! :)

recommendations.name: Tammy Kimbler
recommendations.subtitle: CCO, Exec Team | Perennial Change Agent Scaling Global Food Systems Change via Communications
recommendations.context: Licia managed Tammy directly
recommendations.description: Tammy is unequivocally an expert in natural & organic marketing communications and brand experiences. Under her leadership, General Mills and the Cascadian Farm brand launched a groundbreaking cereal made with Kernza. Without her passion and expertise, our Deeply Rooted idea simply would not have come to life as successfully as it did. The campaign garnered national attention from Fast Company, NPR, Eating Well, and through its compelling brand experience effectively engaged consumers, media, and the food industry in support of the Cascadian Farm mission for climate-positive food.

updates.postText:
C’est avec un réel plaisir que j’ai découvert et pris goût à l’atelier du DeVinci FabLab, dans lequel Yasmine Farhati et moi avons eu l’honneur de concrétiser notre projet. 

Un grand merci à nos deux encadrants Noë Liu et Jacky Kuoch qui ont su nous guider et nous transmettre leur passion pour le FabLab, tout au long du projet !

Je tiens également à remercier Carmina Marcarian pour la réalisation de cet article. 

#EMLV #ESILV #PULV #innovation #FabLab #Imprimante3D

Dans le cadre d’un travail de groupe réalisé au sein de mon école, dont le but était d’inventer un produit ou un concept innovant, Stanislas GREGOIRE, Yasmine Farhati et moi même avons crées CaddyPlus, une application visant à améliorer l’ensemble de vos courses en un scan !

C’est donc dans le cadre de ce projet que nous avons réalisé tous les trois un petit pitch de 1:15, présentant le concept de notre application. Je tiens à remercier Zohra Farhati pour sa voix qui anime parfaitement notre pitch !

 
 #EMLV #PULV #PoleLeonardDeVinci #EcoleDeCommerce #Projet #Innovation #Technologie #Application #Concept #Pitch #Publicité #Vidéo #MieuxManger #Environnement

Dans le cadre d’un travail de groupe réalisé au sein de mon école, dont le but était d’inventer un produit ou un concept innovant, Stanislas GREGOIRE, Yasmine Farhati et moi même avons crée CaddyPlus, une application visant à améliorer l’ensemble de vos courses en un scan ! 

C’est dans le cadre de ce projet que j’ai eu l’occasion de réaliser pour la première fois un prototype d’interface d’application, dont voici les aperçu : 

 
 #EMLV #PULV  #PoleLeonardDeVinci #Technologie #Projet #startup #innovation #InterfaceApplication #mieuxmanger

Voici un petit aperçu des affiches que j’ai eu l’occasion de réaliser durant la semaine soft skills, pour l’association fictive que nous avons crée : « Less Meat, More Life » 🌿🌱

#affiches #emlv #vegetarisme  #mieuxmanger #association 

Frederic Fuchs

https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAGfFbcB.jobTitle: Brand Experience-Content (Sr. Brand Experience Content Planner)
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAGfFbcB.about: I'm a creative connector. I love taking a brief and turning it into engaging earned and paid content for social, TV, audio, commerce and more. I specialize in storytelling through influencer and partnership content development, strengthening consumer love through thoughtful details and elevating a brand's voice. I have a gift for sifting through the day’s internet culture and curating opportunities to show up in culture for every brand I touch. Highly collaborative and fun to work with, let's make something amazing.

experiences.title: Brand Experience-Content (Sr. Brand Experience Content Planner)
experiences.jobDescription:
A creative problem solver and passionate about the consumer content experience, I thrive on both sides of the brief as a creator and a content strategist. 

Snacks- 
For Nature Valley PicturePreservation, I encouraged families to get ‘Out There’ and share their favorite nature spots. Partnering with The National Park Foundation, we received 16.5MM Sweepstakes entries and +100M family nature photos for brand use. Nature Valley won PRNEWS’ Digital Award for Best Visual Storytelling and a Platinum Award for Best Video Series through National Park Conservation Association series (+500MM organic views across all platforms). 
 
Nature Valley ReTok was the first reward-based campaign on TikTok. Using the pillars of Reduce, Reuse and Recycle to unlock prize drops during Earth Month, we received 20B earned impressions, 256MM paid impressions, .4% sales lift and an internal Gold Medal Award. I led creative direction, influencer and social resulting in AdAge recognition as part of their article, “20 Brands Getting Gen Z Attention Right Now.” 
 
For Fiber One’s Fall Off Forgiveness, I wrote “Go Ahead, Eat the Brownie,” and meant it. I developed this two-year campaign, pitched Lisa Ann Walter as talent, wrote a :15 and WW co-branded sweepstakes. The sweeps surpassed PR KPI goals by 225% in media and 40% for social.
 
Baking- Betty Crocker
I created The BettyLab, a STEM-inspired food series to encourage kids to experiment in the kitchen. Partnering with Mattel’s Barbie, we co-created a How-To series, Walmart-exclusive Barbie + Betty Crocker BettyLab kit and an "experiment" series starring Christina Milian. 

Dairy- Oui by Yoplait
I developed the Oui by Yoplait owned content strategy, launching the brand on social with a custom publisher and influencer approach. From the French sayings on the back of every lid to the Oui Heritage Collection concepts of French Country, French Modern and French Chic; I led the foundational creative that continues to make people say, Oui.

experiences.title: Writer, Actor and Social Media Manager
experiences.jobDescription: Late Nights Minneapolis was a staged late-night style talk show and comedy experience backed by a full band. As a group we created a web series called "Writers Roommates," a podcast called "Later Nights" and 18 live shows, 17 at the Phoenix Theater; one in a "haunted" basement.

experiences.title: Digital Content Editor- Meetings + Events Media Group
experiences.jobDescription:
During my time as the Digital Content Editor of the Meetings + Events Media Group, I wrote, edited, curated content, blogged and maintained the websites and social media presences for California, Colorado, Michigan, Minnesota, Mountain, National, Northwest and Texas Meetings + Events Magazines.

I contributed written content for the printed versions of the M+E magazines such as the Meeting Notes and Industry Updates sections and could be seen behind the scenes capturing moments for the Instagram, Twitter and various social media accounts. I was also a featured speaker at SPINCon 2013 with my presentation, "Social Anxiety: Selecting Social Media Networks, Curating Content and Engaging Your Digital Community Like a Pro."

During this time I also absorbed as many photo shoots as possible and dipped my toe into the content creation waters.

Strategized, negotiated, and executed to drive efficiencies and best in class practices.  Responsible for negotiations, pricing, and logistics coordination of a dozen products into a dozen plants, accounting for over 500 million pounds of product annually. 

•	Implemented mass efficiencies to internal and external reporting of financial and physical coverage, market intelligence, logistics pipelines, and pricing models.

•	Articulated financial data around constantly evolving purchasing and hedging strategies to executive leadership, customers, and internal sales teams.

•	Solved problems proactively, thinking outside the box and creating innovative solutions to challenges arising in market moves, price fluctuations, logistics structures, production capacities, and customer promotions. Developed synergies across communication channels.

experiences.title: Commodity Purchasing: Grain Origination Analyst, Tyson Local Grain Services
experiences.jobDescription:
•Managed the development and integration of the new Tyson Local Grain Services program aimed at recruiting farmers nationwide to sell corn directly off the farm to Tyson, accounting for over $4,000,000 annual cost savings. I designed and coordinated the development of online resource functionality to enable local producers to sell grain to Tyson Foods. Engaged with farmers and managed those relationships, acting as a consultant with existing customers and obtaining new customers. Exceptional capacity to collaborate, communicate, and develop synergies with customers and team members. Initiated over one hundred individual customer meetings, in addition to planning and representing Tyson at eleven promotional events for Local Grain Services in FY2016. Communicated strategy and success to executive leadership and other Tyson stakeholders. Forward thinking, engaging, creative, and leadership oriented.

•Created a targeted marketing strategy and related advertising initiatives to develop the Tyson Local Grain Services brand. Maintained social media accounts, designed, trademarked, and produced branding material, and implemented multi-media advertising campaigns with FarmJournal, Tomorrow's Top Producer and the Farmers’ Almanac. 

•Traded commodity futures through the Chicago Board of Trade. Executed multi-million dollar trades on a regular basis, implementing strategy to hedge risk associated with the forward purchase of feed ingredient commodities.

•Created contracts accounting for over 22,000,000 bushels of corn purchased through Tyson Local Grain Services in FY2016, shifting the purchasing matrix to reduce rail corn volume by 30% and implementing cost savings of $3,000,000 in FY2016. 

•Articulated money market data and insights for an audience of 400 Tyson internal customers, including executive leadership, through a weekly dashboard providing information around currencies, commodities, and world equity and bond markets.

I have worked for Heather as a Marketing Intern since she first joined Aramark. During this, she has been a great manager, boss, and co-worker. I have worked with her on a variety of marketing and promotional programs and feel that is she is very creative, a strong leader, motivated, and in-tune with her target market. One strong element of Heather’s personality is that she thinks outside the box, looking beyond traditional means of marketing and promotions and devising new and creative methods of reaching students. 
As a manger, Heather is: friendly, organized, personable, creative, a strong leader, well-spoken, goal oriented, flexible, and much more.
It is a wonderful experience working with her and she has taught me many valuable lessons.

recommendations.subtitle: Community Marketing I Strategic Leader I Project Manager
recommendations.context: Iuliana reported directly to Suzanne
recommendations.description:
I have worked for Suzanne for two years as a Marketing Intern reporting directly to her. During these two years, she has been a great manager, boss, and role model and it has been a pleasure to work with her. During my tenure with Aramark, she has taught me a great deal about marketing, promotions, business communications, and the management of a multi-million dollar account. Personally, she has helped to develop my organization and interpersonal skills.  Some of the marketing programs I have assisted her with include: the launch of the sustainability program on campus; recruitment, training and management of Green Team and Food Dude members; special promotions and events such as “Eat Well. Live Well;” market research; and new product and location launches.  
As a manger, Suzanne is: friendly, organized, personable, creative, a strong leader, well-spoken, goal oriented, flexible, time oriented and much more.
Suzanne has been a wonderful mentor these two years, and it is in part due to her help and the opportunities she has afforded me that I have my current job offer after graduation.

recommendationsReceived.name: Shaina Antila
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Team Lead + Sr. UX Architect || Empathy | Harmony | Relator | Competition | Restorative
recommendationsReceived.context: Amanda was senior to Shaina but didn't manage Shaina directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
There are few people a person works with in their lifetime that leave an impact on them. I had the pleasure of such impact, while working with Amanda at LAKANA. 

As a budding designer, I could always look to Amanda for guidance, design feedback, and support. Amanda is skilled in many areas (from marketing to UX), detail oriented, innovative, and always strives for the best solution. Amanda is a pleasure to work with as she is professional, positive, and confident in her work. She has the power to succeed through chaos and change, and always does so while remaining focused and with a clear head. 

I only wish that I would have the opportunity to work with Amanda again in the future!

recommendationsReceived.name: Jason Walkow
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Co-founder at Quin
recommendationsReceived.context: Amanda was senior to Jason but didn't manage Jason directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
Amanda was always my go-to person for anything and everything design. Whether it was quick feedback or direction on a brand-new concept, I could always rely on Amanda to come up with something game-changing. In my career, I've never come across such a capable and inspiring designer with such a wonderful sense of humor and sunny disposition. 

Even on a large design team, I could always tell when a design concept came from Amanda. She has a signature style and brand that I totally fell in love with, and strived to incorporate in my own designs. And if this wasn't enough, she is also one of the nicest and most open people I have ever worked with. I was never afraid to bug her for help or feedback, and she was always completely respectful of my own ideas when it came to things she was working on. That sort of collaborative spirit is something that can be incredibly rare with designers, and she has it in loads. 

In the future, whenever I have the chance to bring a designer in on a project, she will undoubtedly be the first (and only) person I think of.

recommendationsReceived.name: Steven Kudirka
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Principal Software Engineer at Teamworks
recommendationsReceived.context: Steven was senior to Amanda but didn't manage Amanda directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Amanda is the consummate professional. Talented, detail-oriented, and demonstrates a passion for not just great, but proper design. She will let you know right away if a design aspect will not equate to a good user experience. She has great instincts and she stays updated with the latest trends in modern UI and UX. During design reviews, Amanda demonstrates valuable and most importantly trusted feedback. She knows what she is talking about. I highly recommend.

She is a very adept thinker, and excellent at managing projects and deadlines. While working with her I have observed her design process, what she creates, and how she communicates her designs, and it has taught me a great deal. She is an asset to any design team.

During our time together, I watched her navigate our team through numerous crucial projects filled with unknowns, complexities, and ambiguity—all while maintaining a positive attitude and upbeat energy.

She is a reliable partner who frequently goes beyond her official responsibilities to ensure the success of the project and team. I appreciate that she’s always looking for opportunities for improvement, growth, and efficiency while driving projects forward.

Allie is not just a fantastic project manager but also a genuine teammate and person. Any team would be lucky to have her!

recommendations.name: Shaina Antila
recommendations.subtitle: Team Lead + Sr. UX Architect || Empathy | Harmony | Relator | Competition | Restorative
recommendations.context: Amanda was senior to Shaina but didn't manage Shaina directly
recommendations.description:
Shaina and I worked together at LAKANA, where she joined the team as a budding designer/developer. I remember being beyond impressed with her maturity and professionalism even then, as she was able to navigate some unique challenges presented to her at the time.

More recently, I've been excited to see her pursue her career growth and goals. Shaina is smart. She is well-spoken, analytical, and is extremely independent with strong leadership qualities. She has an extremely adaptable skillset and is consistently looking for new ways to learn + grow. Her ambition to push herself to the be the best she can be inspires me in my own professional journey.

She's passionate about what she does, and is one of the few people in my network that I personally know, trust, and would recommend without hesitation. She's a great asset to any team, and I look forward to the opportunity to work with her again in the future!

recommendations.name: Jason Walkow

I had the pleasure of working with Jason on the design team at LAKANA. His infectious positivity and upbeat attitude made working with him effortless and fun. He has strong front end development skills, and was always patiently willing to explain technical concepts to me - helping bridge the gap between design and development.

He works hard, and has fun doing it.

Jason is dependable, creative, and talented, and will be a huge asset to any team. I’d recommend him in a heartbeat and hope to work with him again in the future!

He is inquisitive and approaches problems with thoughtfulness and care, making him a great collaborator.

Always eager to help and teach, Justin is one of the hardest working people I’ve had the pleasure of being on a team with and I would hands-down recommend him.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAADccgsB.headline: I help brands build the foundation that makes great creative possible | Brand Strategy & Positioning | Former General Mills
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAADccgsB.about:
When a brand knows exactly who it is, everything gets easier. The brief. The creative. The results.

I've spent 20 years figuring out why some brands make work that lasts and others make work that's forgotten by Friday. The difference almost never comes down to the idea. It comes down to the foundation underneath it.

For the last decade at General Mills, I led brand strategy and creative development for Gushers, Fruit Roll-Ups, Chex Mix, and Mott's. I've repositioned brands, launched campaigns that earned billions of impressions, and built the kind of strategic clarity that keeps good work alive through every round of feedback.

That's what I do now as an independent consultant. I help brands get clear on three things: who they are at their core, who they're actually for, and how they want to show up in the world. Once those are true, the brief writes itself. The creative has somewhere to stand. The work gets better and stays better.

I'm available for brand foundation and positioning work, campaign development, creative leadership, and ongoing strategic partnership.

steph@stephlensing.com | stephlensing.com

experiences.title: Creative Strategist & Consultant
experiences.jobDescription:
I help agencies and brands protect bold creative work through the messy middle - the approval process where good ideas get watered down.

With 20+ years across both agency and client side, I know what kills great creative: personal bias overriding strategy, quarterly earnings pressure, MBA ego, weak brand foundations, and brands trying to mirror customers instead of leading them.

I partner with marketing leaders to build brand strategy, develop campaigns, and provide senior-level creative direction that survives the approval gauntlet.

Recent work: AdAge Campaign of the Year (Gushers FruitHead), ADWEEK Media Plan of the Year, Webby Award, campaigns generating billions of earned impressions.

Available for project-based work, retainers, and fractional leadership.

updates.postText:
I've been noticing a pattern over the last 10 years: brands spend millions on campaigns, hire talented agencies, and then the work gets softened or killed in the approval process.

Not because the idea is bad. Because someone in legal wants to adjust the tagline, finance questions the media spend, or VPs who weren't in the creative reviews suddenly have thoughts. The bold idea gets watered down until it's "safe". And safe doesn't move anyone.

So I'm doing something about it.

Steph Lensing Creative Consulting is officially live!

I help brands and agencies protect bold creative through the approval process. Creative strategy, campaign development, fractional creative leadership. Coaching teams on how to give feedback that makes the work better, not just different.

I spent 10 years at General Mills protecting work like Gushers FruitHead and the Fruit Roll-Ups ice cream trend. Now I'm helping other brands and agencies make sure good ideas don't die in "the messy middle."

If you're launching something big, rebranding, or working with an agency and want to make sure the work survives execution, let's talk! If you're a creative friend and want help navigating the corporate approval process, let's talk! 

www.stephlensing.com

I'm excited to share some Big News: I’ve officially jumped into freelancing.

After years of building brands inside big organizations, I’m excited to build something of my own. I’m partnering with brands and agencies on brand strategy, creative leadership, and marketing work, helping teams move fast and find their spark.

If you’re in a busy season, just won new business, launching a new product, or just need an extra brain in the mix, I’d love to connect!

Cheers to 2026!
How Fruit Roll-Ups’ TikTok strategy helped a viral trend happen With the brand, not to the brand

https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAM7e-sB.jobTitle: Sr. Planner, Brand Experience - Content Production
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ACwAAAM7e-sB.about:
I build structure around creative ambition.

I’m a producer, creative operator, and storyteller who translates vision into execution at scale. With more than 20 years in production and project leadership, I work at the intersection of creativity and operational excellence—guiding complex initiatives from concept to rollout with clarity, collaboration, and momentum.

I’ve led large-scale photography and video productions, managed cross-functional teams, built scalable workflows, and overseen vendor partnerships and budgets across agency and enterprise environments. I thrive in fast-moving spaces where ideas are big, timelines are tight, and alignment matters.

What differentiates me is simple:

I understand both the art and the infrastructure.

I know how to protect creative vision while building the systems that make it executable, repeatable, and scalable. I turn complexity into forward motion and create environments where teams feel supported, focused, and inspired to do their best work.

I’m also a lifelong performer and vocalist, which shapes how I lead—with presence, empathy, strong communication, and a deep awareness of audience connection. Whether directing a production or aligning stakeholders around a shared strategy, I bring steadiness, warmth, and decisive leadership.

What drives me now:

• Building creative ecosystems where talent thrives
• Designing operational systems that unlock creative freedom
• Producing work that balances brand strategy with genuine humanity
• Bridging vision and execution with clarity
• Leading with kindness, curiosity, and confidence

recommendationsReceived.context: Sara was senior to Jill but didn't manage Jill directly
recommendationsReceived.description:
Jill is, simply put, the most positive person I have ever worked with.  (seriously)  Her enthusiasm is contagious -- and you leave every encounter feeling valued and energized. 

Also, Jill's time in front of the camera (or on the stage) has made her an invaluable producer.  While working behind the scenes, Jill has consistently demonstrated the ability to anticipate everyone's needs: from the client -- to the talent -- to the photographer -- to the guy parking the car.  She has the innate ability to pull pieces together to produce a flawless work of art -- of any medium.  

Jill is super-talented, super-fun, and a super-valued member of any team.  And I sincerely hope our professional paths cross again soon!

recommendationsReceived.description:
Okay...you have NEVER met anyone quite like Jill before. Once you do, you will be changed...in the best way possible.

She exudes a fantastic sense of "can do", energy and passion.

Working with her you believe that anything is possible. She is committed, driven, savvy and professional.

She is the mistress of details, able to juggle everything (and I mean everything) yet..nothing drops.  That is a true natural talent. Her ability to maneuver the in's and out's of the job while managing talent is remarkable.

But I think her greatest talent lies in working with people. All types of people. Regardless of your attitude, your demeanor, your personality, Jill worked her magic.  Moving people in and out of the process. Guiding them, coaching them, leading them. She has a special skill. To be able to work with every type of individual imaginable, yet still accomplish the end result. She is gifted.

I truly enjoyed working with Jill and would welcome the opportunity to do so again.

Quite the Character =)  

From my first day at Marketing Architects, Scott was a huge advocate for me and for the company.  He was inspired, enthused, and such a believer in what I was doing and what this company was doing.  Scott has a way with words that just makes you commit and want to work hard for the cause.

I think we would all agree that having Scott be our first connection made at Marketing Architects was such a huge part of why we wanted to be here, why we wanted to work hard for the company and why we were excited about this new adventure at MA.  

Scott is a coach, a trainer, a friend, a leader, and an example to all as someone who loves what he does, is darn good at it, and wants to share that passion with others.  I would highly recommend Scott for any position and believe that any company would be lucky to have him as an advocate and asset to their team.

Happy to share with this community my new professional adventure: Today, after two years of being International Marketing Manager at Goiko, I'm leaving the company and joining General Mills as Sr. Brand Manager - Haagen Dazs Shops. 
I would like to publicly thank my amazing team at Goiko
Andoni Goicoechea and María Jesús García San José: Thanks for trusting my capabilities and letting me lead important strategic projects for the brand. 
Juan Manuel Sánchez Martínez Thanks for all the strategic knowledge and support.
The beloved content team led by the one and only Elisa Molina, for always being there on top of the wave.
And last but not least my International team of cracks: Eduardo Camiña,  Miguelangel Nascimento Rodriguez and Héctor Maiolino. Without you guys this 2 years would have been less fun and rewarding. 
I have learnt from Goiko a lot: the importance of the immediacy, the value of teamwork and above all that when you do things the right way everything is possible. 
I’m sure great things await for Goiko: the growth and success will continue because when you see and feel all that passion in the team, nothing less could come up from that.

I'm a bit sad to be leaving this wonderful company and looking forward to being part of another great company such as General Mills.

Two weeks ago, we welcomed summer and launched our new Häagen-Dazs shops summer menu with a nice rooftop party surrounded by the best Madrid views. The name of the event? Häagen Bar the only place where  best things on earth are mixed: cocktails and ice cream. 
For this project I had the chance to work with an amazing team: The international Häagen-Dazs  team that envisioned this event and gave us all the support and help to make it happen; the local support team that were always there to help and solve any last minute issue, also amazing partners such as PRGarage in charge of invitations and follow ups with press and influencers, Brandcrops in charge of the Social Media Management and always on content, HMY for the event production and last but not least thanks to our wonderful mixologist Adriana Chia that did an amazing job mixing our ice cream with top spirits. 
Here I share with you a glimpse of that wonderful June afternoon, full of nice people, music and of course our summer shops creations. 

If after watching this video, you don’t want to taste a Mai Tai or a Piña Colada Milkshake in one of our shops… I don’t know where your sense of taste is. 🤤
Find your closest store here!  ➡️ https://lnkd.in/dC83V6a 

#Event #HaagenBar #HaagenDazsExperience #HaagenDazsShops
Maria Gabriela Pallotta Ortega, Sophie Duval, Miguel León Peña, Alice Hodgson, Aurélie Lory

updates.postText:
Welp… it’s that time again. Time for my annual LinkedIn post 😅

But this one’s a fun one—because I just hit 3 YEARS at General Mills (what?!) and I’ve officially been promoted to BX Planner for K-12 and Non-Commercial Channels! 🎉

What started as me just “helping out” during a team gap quickly turned into a new opportunity!

I’ve gotten to work on some super fun design projects, lead big initiatives, and support the K-12 and Non-Com teams that are truly doing meaningful (and slightly chaotic) work. I’m so thankful for the people who encouraged me, challenged me, and let me design my little heart out!

Here’s to year 4 at GM, a new role, and maybe—just maybe—posting on LinkedIn again before 2026. 🚀

2 years at General Mills 🎉

Thankful for great coworkers, work Bible studies & lots of creative projects. Can’t complain about the free cereal either 🥣 #blessed

1st year at General Mills done #MissionAccomplished
👊 Goodbye Sales
👋 Hello Brand Experience (promoted to content coordinator)

I'm not going to lie; as a creative individual working in data analytics; it was an EXTREMELY humbling year. However, I am happy to announce that I am now a professional at Microsoft Excel. Thats all. Excited to be working in my strengths now 💪 

Thank you for helping me through my first year Mark Skeba & Tyler Moon

Proof that you don’t need to go to some big business school to get your dream job 🥳

I am grateful to have attended North Central University and can’t wait to bring my experience & enthusiasm to General Mills.

Thank you to my phenomenal professors, who always pushed and encouraged me! (Mark Skeba Neely Tamminga Dr. Brian Stewart Kjrk Reyerson Shawn Sauve 

Thank you to my boss, who allowed me to lead and grow (Gregg Katchmark)

Thank you to all my competitive classmates who brought the best out of me (you know who you are 😉)

& one final thank you to Bill Tibbetts for always rooting for me!

 #newjob #business #university #thankyou

It’s not every day your graphic design work is on America’s #1 National Good Morning Show, but when it is, it’s for a good cause! 

For the past few months, I’ve been honored to be a part of the Engage Your Destiny team as their marketing designer to promote the Heroes Honor Festival. We’re excited to be at Daytona International Speedway this May 27 & 28 to bring honor to all the Vietnam veterans and the sacrifice they made for our country. 

If you’re ready for the BIGGEST, BADDEST, MOST PATRIOTIC CELEBRATION, you need to check it out! https://lnkd.in/dWYXnQin

 #marketing #graphicdesign #veterans

experiences.title: Associate Manager – Brand Experience, Carbe Diem!™
Associate Manager – Brand Experience, Good Measure™ Blood Sugar-Friendly Foods
experiences.jobDescription:
Good Measure™ was the first start-up from the General Mills Venture Studio. The brand pioneered the blood sugar-friendly category of snacks, which are clinically studied to have little impact on blood sugar levels.

• Owned and managed creative, influencer, PR, social, partnerships, and experiential workstreams, along with the full Consumer budget.
• Developed omnichannel communication and experience strategies to drive distribution and volume across 10 retailers and 8,500+ stores.
• Led an integrated Paid, Earned, and Owned Media plan, delivering consistent quarterly growth across retail, natural/organic, drug channels, DTC, and Amazon.
• Pioneered new partnership capabilities for General Mills, launching a brand-led network of 1K+ Registered Dietitians and a Scientific Advisory Board with Mayo Clinic.
• Oversaw 15+ scopes of work across 4 agency partners, ensuring all consumer communications aligned with the brand promise: “Made with Care, Made for You.”
• Co-developed Good Measure’s brand identity, leading internal efforts and collaborating with creative agencies to build a distinctive Brand World that informed visual strategy, tone of voice, packaging, and Owned & Paid Media.
• Played a key role in early innovation work to inform category expansion, contributing to focus groups and qualitative research that shaped pipeline strategy and new product development.
• Served as dotted line manager to Creative Design Manager.
• Operated as a direct strategic partner to the General Manager.

recommendationsReceived.name: Ellyn Minnick
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Design Leader + Team Builder | Making Brands & People Shine
recommendationsReceived.context: Ellyn worked with Hanna on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I had the pleasure of working with Hanna, a highly talented Brand Experience and Marketing Manager. Her creativity, strategic thinking, and dedication to excellence consistently stood out. Hanna has a remarkable ability to understand market trends and consumer behavior, which she seamlessly translates into innovative marketing strategies and compelling brand experiences.

Hanna's leadership and collaborative spirit foster a positive and productive work environment. She excels in managing cross-functional teams, ensuring that every project is executed flawlessly and delivers outstanding results. Her keen eye for detail and her passion for brand storytelling are truly inspiring.

Hanna's contributions have significantly enhanced our brand's presence and engagement. She is a visionary marketer with a proven track record of success. If you have the chance to work with her, DO IT!

recommendationsReceived.name: Lindsay Naple
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: eCommerce Consultant & RN
recommendationsReceived.context: Lindsay worked with Hanna on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: I had the pleasure of collaborating with Hanna for over a year and can easily say it was one of the most productive, positive work relationships I've had with a colleague. We've worked on several campaigns together and effectively integrated Social with eCommerce for a more unified, consistent Brand experience. Hanna excels because she brings both positive energy and a strong worth ethic to the table. She is incredibly articulate and thoughtful about the ideas, initiatives, feedback, and visions she shares with a team. While she is great to work with in a collaborative setting she is also very capable and willing to head a project or campaign on her own. Hanna's strong communications skills and friendly demeanor also allow her to successfully manage brand partnerships and influencer relationships. I know first-hand the people she works with are often wanting to maintain the relationship and collaborate together again in the future. Additionally Hanna's strong creative skills are an asset to a Brand's content creation and copywriting. She takes the time to strategically craft her vision and theme to the Brand's voice persona, not her own, allowing for authentic, meaningful connections with a Brand's fanbase. I strongly recommend Hanna and would work with her again in a heartbeat.

recommendationsReceived.description: Hanna is an absolute pleasure. She brings a strong work ethic, warm and positive attitude, and service orientation to her work, others, and the organization. recommendations.name: Ellyn Minnick
recommendations.subtitle: Design Leader + Team Builder | Making Brands & People Shine
recommendations.context: Hanna was senior to Ellyn but didn't manage Ellyn directly
recommendations.description:
I had the pleasure of collaborating with Ellyn during our time at Good Measure, the first start-up from the General Mills Venture Studio.

Ellyn led all brand design, defending, enforcing, and optimizing creative to drive brand equity through digital, social, and experiential channels. Her major contributions included establishing Good Measure's brand world, visual identity, and packaging design. Additionally, Ellyn excels at leading cross-functional teams, engaging brand leadership, external agency partners, and internal consultants to drive better decisions and results with agility.

Ellyn's work played a key role in Good Measure's rapid growth across various retail channels, landing Good Measure in 8,500 locations nationwide, including natural/organic and drug stores. Her unparalleled creativity, efficiency, and accountability make her an invaluable asset, and I wholeheartedly recommend her for any role requiring design leadership and a commitment to brand excellence.

Though new to the world of better-for-you CPG, Sol Bee became an asset to the team. She is not only a quick study, but also adaptable, insightful, and extremely talented. Sol Bee was instrumental in executing Dang’s brand refresh, Natural Products Expo West coordination, and packaging updates. She was also a valued creative contributor — concepting and executing 3 vibrant brand videos that will be used across social + e-commerce platforms. One of these videos drove 85% of post traffic to dangfoods.com, making it a top-performer on Instagram. For all of these reasons, I am confident in Sol Bee’s future success and ability to excel as a marketer.

recommendations.context: Hanna reported directly to Brian
recommendations.description: During my time with Cargill, I watched Brian effectively lead a team, consistently make smart marketing decisions, think with a creative edge and stay up to speed on channel development and digital trends. Under Brian’s leadership, Truvia successfully launched a new product and established a fine-tuned digital/social marketing strategy; he was an invaluable addition to our team. As my manager, Brian met me with patience, encouragement, an awesome sense of humor and mentorship – it was a pleasure working together!

recommendations.name: Greg Holmes, MA
recommendations.subtitle: Driving High-Impact Talent Acquisition for Global Organizations
recommendations.context: Hanna was Greg's client
recommendations.description: I’ve known Greg for nearly two years, and it has been a real joy to work together. He is knowledgeable, reachable, trustworthy, encouraging, and will always be his clients' advocate. Greg goes above and beyond the responsibilities in his job title, and turns recruiting into relationship building. I’ve recommended his services to my friends and colleagues - whether it be for full-time or freelance work. Greg has connected me with gigs that were not only perfect for my background and skill set, but also complimentary to my interests and passions. With his help, I was able to take my career to the next level.

experiences.title: Head of Social Media and Customer Services
experiences.jobDescription:
Wauwaa is a London and Helsinki based online destination that combines content, online shopping and community to deliver new parents and growing families a unique 'browse and discover' experience for all their parenting needs. Whilst at Wauwaa I’ve progressed from Social Media Intern to Online Community Manager and now hold the position Head of Social Media and Customer Services. Alongside two Social Media Assistants my team now includes a Customer Service Representative. 

Working within an eCommerce Startup means my role is extremely fast paced and very varied. This type of environment appeals to my hardworking, go-getter attitude and allows me to find that perfect balance between creative and strategic thinking, analysing social media statistics alongside brainstorming and initiating creative campaigns that drive both engagement and conversions.

experiences.title: Head of Social Media
experiences.jobDescription:
My role includes managing the brands social identity, whilst also helping to grow Wauwaa’s customer base and generating sales through Social Advertising. I manage a team of two part-time Social Media Assistants who connect with customers through a variety of Social channels on a daily basis, including Facebook, Twitter (@wauwaa), Pinterest, YouTube, Instagram and Google+. As a team we run online campaigns and competitions regularly, ensuring we have appropriate Social Media strategies across all our channels. 

 Working at Wauwaa has enhanced my customer service skills and taught me how to interact online in a friendly and professional manner, building up great relationships between companies and external customers, suppliers and other businesses. My passion for working in eCommerce stems from my own love of online shopping alongside my belief that online retail is key in the future of retail success.
